content.text:
Design a Workforce Learning & Procurement Reconciliation Portal to let organizations manage employee learning paths alongside vendor order governance and settlement in a fully offline environment. Users include Learners, Procurement Specialists, Approvers, Finance Analysts, Content Moderators, and System Administrators. The React web UI provides local username/password sign-in, optional MFA via time-based one-time passwords generated in an authenticator app, and role-based menus that only reveal permitted pages and actions. Learners can search a unified library of internal resources and job/skill tags using fast on-site full-text search with typo tolerance, optional synonym and pinyin matching, and multi-dimensional filters for date, category, and tags; results can be sorted by relevance, popularity, or most recent, with clear “why recommended” cues. The experience supports intelligent recommendations driven by viewing/completion behavior and tags, including cold-start defaults by job family, deduplication across near-duplicate resources, and diversity controls that ensure no more than 40% of a recommendation carousel comes from a single category. Learners enroll in learning paths with required/elective rules (for example, 6 required items plus any 2 electives) and track progress down to each resource, with resume learning, cross-device sync within the same offline network, and export of learning records to a downloadable CSV. Procurement and Finance users review orders and disputes through a guided workflow: ratings with text and image attachments, merchant replies, negative-review appeals with evidence uploads, and arbitration outcomes that control whether a review is hidden, shown with a disclaimer, or restored.

The backend uses Go with Echo to expose REST-style APIs consumed by the decoupled React frontend, and PostgreSQL persists all business data locally, including users, roles/permissions, learning paths, resource progress events, job/skill taxonomy, reviews, disputes, billing rules, and settlement ledgers. Search indexing is built and maintained on-premise: tokenization, weighting, and fuzzy match are executed locally, with nightly rebuilds and incremental updates on content changes; archive pages are auto-generated by month and tag based on persisted publish dates. The job/skill taxonomy supports hierarchical relationships, synonym consolidation, and conflict detection rules (for example, blocking two active synonyms pointing to different canonical tags), with an admin review queue and audit trails. Reconciliation supports configurable warehouse and transportation billing rules, generation of AR/AP entries, statement comparison, variance write-off thresholds (for example, variances under $5.00 auto-suggest write-off but still require Finance approval), settlement status transitions, and cost allocation by department and cost center, with exports compatible with downstream finance systems via offline file drop or LAN-based webhooks. Security includes salted password hashing, encryption at rest for sensitive fields (such as recovery secrets and dispute evidence metadata), masking of personal data in non-admin views, configurable session timeouts (15 minutes idle, 8 hours max), and a centralized configuration center with feature flags, parameters, phased rollout by user role, scheduled jobs with retry/compensation, and app version compatibility checks that block unsupported clients while allowing read-only access for up to 14 days.
Let me start by exploring the codebase structure to understand what we're working with.

1	package main
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"log"
7		"os"
8		"os/signal"
9		"syscall"
10		"time"
11	
12		"wlpr-portal/internal/handlers"
13		"wlpr-portal/internal/middleware"
14		"wlpr-portal/internal/repository"
15		"wlpr-portal/internal/services"
16		"wlpr-portal/pkg/crypto"
17		"wlpr-portal/pkg/jwt"
18	
19		"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool"
20		"github.com/labstack/echo/v4"
21		echomw "github.com/labstack/echo/v4/middleware"
22	)
23	
24	func main() {
25		// Initialize encryption key
26		if err := crypto.InitEncryptionKey(); err != nil {
27			log.Fatalf("Failed to init encryption key: %v", err)
28		}
29	
30		// Initialize JWT secret
31		if err := jwt.InitJWTSecret(); err != nil {
32			log.Fatalf("Failed to init JWT secret: %v", err)
33		}
34	
35		// Database connection
36		dbURL := os.Getenv("DATABASE_URL")
37		if dbURL == "" {
38			dbURL = "postgres://wlpr:wlpr_secret@localhost:5432/wlpr_portal?sslmode=disable"
39		}
40	
41		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
42		defer cancel()
43	
44		dbPool, err := pgxpool.New(ctx, dbURL)
45		if err != nil {
46			log.Fatalf("Failed to connect to database: %v", err)
47		}
48		defer dbPool.Close()
49	
50		if err := dbPool.Ping(ctx); err != nil {
51			log.Fatalf("Failed to ping database: %v", err)
52		}
53		log.Println("Connected to PostgreSQL")
54	
55		// Repositories
56		userRepo := repository.NewUserRepository(dbPool)
57		configRepo := repository.NewConfigRepository(dbPool)
58		taxonomyRepo := repository.NewTaxonomyRepository(dbPool)
59		searchRepo := repository.NewSearchRepository(dbPool)
60		learningRepo := repository.NewLearningRepository(dbPool)
61	
62		// Services
63		configService := services.NewConfigService(configRepo)
64		if err := configService.LoadAll(ctx); err != nil {
65			log.Fatalf("Failed to load initial configs: %v", err)
66		}
67		configService.StartBackgroundSync(ctx, 30*time.Second)
68	
69		authService := services.NewAuthService(userRepo, configRepo)
70		mfaService := services.NewMFAService(userRepo)
71		searchService := services.NewSearchService(searchRepo, taxonomyRepo, configService)
72		learningService := services.NewLearningService(learningRepo, searchRepo)
73		procRepo := repository.NewProcurementRepository(dbPool)
74		reconService := services.NewReconciliationService(procRepo, configService)
75		exportSinkService := services.NewExportSinkService(configService)
76	
77		// Start recommendation worker (nightly rebuild, runs every 24h)
78		recWorker := services.NewRecommendationWorker(learningRepo, searchRepo, configService)
79		recWorker.Start(ctx, 24*time.Hour)
80	
81		// Handlers
82		authHandler := handlers.NewAuthHandler(authService, mfaService, userRepo)
83		configHandler := handlers.NewConfigHandler(configService)
84		searchHandler := handlers.NewSearchHandler(searchService)
85		taxonomyHandler := handlers.NewTaxonomyHandler(taxonomyRepo)
86		learningHandler := handlers.NewLearningHandler(learningService)
87		procHandler := handlers.NewProcurementHandler(procRepo, reconService, exportSinkService)
88	
89		// Middleware
90		authMW := middleware.NewAuthMiddleware(authService, configService)
91	
92		// Echo setup
93		e := echo.New()
94		e.HideBanner = true
95	
96		// Global middleware
97		e.Use(echomw.Recover())
98		e.Use(middleware.RequestLogger())
99		e.Use(echomw.CORSWithConfig(echomw.CORSConfig{
100			AllowOrigins:     []string{"http://localhost:5173", "http://localhost:3000"},
101			AllowMethods:     []string{"GET", "POST", "PUT", "PATCH", "DELETE", "OPTIONS"},
102			AllowHeaders:     []string{"Authorization", "Content-Type", "X-App-Version"},
103			ExposeHeaders:    []string{"X-App-Deprecated", "X-Min-Version"},
104			AllowCredentials: true,
105		}))
106		e.Use(authMW.AppVersionCheck())
107	
108		// Health check
109		e.GET("/api/health", func(c echo.Context) error {
110			return c.JSON(200, map[string]string{"status": "ok"})
111		})
112	
113		// Public auth routes (no JWT required)
114		auth := e.Group("/api/auth")
115		auth.POST("/login", authHandler.Login)
116		auth.POST("/register", authHandler.Register)
117		auth.POST("/mfa/verify", authHandler.VerifyMFA)
118	
119		// Protected auth routes
120		authProtected := e.Group("/api/auth", authMW.RequireAuth())
121		authProtected.POST("/logout", authHandler.Logout)
122		authProtected.GET("/me", authHandler.Me)
123		authProtected.POST("/mfa/setup", authHandler.SetupMFA)
124		authProtected.POST("/mfa/confirm", authHandler.ConfirmMFA)
125		authProtected.POST("/mfa/disable", authHandler.DisableMFA)
126	
127		// Admin user management (system_admin only)
128		adminGroup := e.Group("/api/admin", authMW.RequireAuth(),
129			authMW.RequireRole("system_admin"))
130		adminGroup.GET("/users", authHandler.ListUsers)
131		adminGroup.POST("/users/assign-role", authHandler.AssignRole)
132	
133		// Config routes (admin only)
134		configGroup := e.Group("/api/config",
135			authMW.RequireAuth(),
136			authMW.RequireRole("system_admin"),
137		)
138		configGroup.GET("/all", configHandler.GetAllConfigs)
139		configGroup.GET("/:key", configHandler.GetConfig)
140		configGroup.PUT("/:key", configHandler.UpdateConfig)
141		configGroup.GET("/flags", configHandler.GetAllFlags)
142		configGroup.GET("/flags/:key", configHandler.GetFlag)
143		configGroup.PUT("/flags/:key", configHandler.UpdateFlag)
144		configGroup.GET("/flags/:key/check", configHandler.CheckFlag)
145	
146		// Public config check (for feature flag evaluation by authenticated users)
147		flagCheck := e.Group("/api/flags", authMW.RequireAuth())
148		flagCheck.GET("/:key/check", configHandler.CheckFlag)
149	
150		// ================================================================
151		// MODULE 2: Catalog & Taxonomy
152		// Learner: Read (search/browse). ContentMod: CRUD. Admin: CRUD.
153		// Finance/Procurement: No access.
154		// ================================================================
155		catalogRead := e.Group("/api", authMW.RequireAuth(),
156			authMW.RequireRole("learner", "content_moderator", "system_admin"))
157		catalogRead.GET("/search", searchHandler.Search)
158		catalogRead.GET("/resources/:id", searchHandler.GetResource)
159		catalogRead.GET("/archives", searchHandler.GetArchives)
160	
161		taxRead := e.Group("/api/taxonomy", authMW.RequireAuth(),
162			authMW.RequireRole("learner", "content_moderator", "system_admin"))
163		taxRead.GET("/tags", taxonomyHandler.GetTags)
164		taxRead.GET("/tags/hierarchy", taxonomyHandler.GetHierarchy)
165		taxRead.GET("/tags/:id", taxonomyHandler.GetTag)
166		taxRead.GET("/synonyms/:tag_id", taxonomyHandler.GetSynonyms)
167	
168		taxWrite := e.Group("/api/taxonomy", authMW.RequireAuth(),
169			authMW.RequireRole("content_moderator", "system_admin"))
170		taxWrite.POST("/tags", taxonomyHandler.CreateTag)
171		taxWrite.POST("/synonyms", taxonomyHandler.CreateSynonym)
172		taxWrite.GET("/review-queue", taxonomyHandler.GetReviewQueue)
173	
174		// ================================================================
175		// MODULE 1: Learning & Development
176		// Learner: Read (assigned/public), Update (own progress/enroll).
177		// ContentMod: CRUD (resources & paths). Admin: CRUD (all).
178		// Finance/Procurement: No access.
179		// ================================================================
180		learnRead := e.Group("/api/learning", authMW.RequireAuth(),
181			authMW.RequireRole("learner", "content_moderator", "system_admin"))
182		learnRead.GET("/paths", learningHandler.GetPaths)
183		learnRead.GET("/paths/:id", learningHandler.GetPath)
184		learnRead.GET("/recommendations", learningHandler.GetRecommendations)
185	
186		// Learner can enroll/update own progress/export own records
187		learnSelf := e.Group("/api/learning", authMW.RequireAuth(),
188			authMW.RequireRole("learner", "content_moderator", "system_admin"))
189		learnSelf.POST("/enroll", learningHandler.Enroll)
190		learnSelf.DELETE("/enroll/:path_id", learningHandler.DropEnrollment)
191		learnSelf.GET("/enrollments", learningHandler.GetEnrollments)
192		learnSelf.GET("/enrollments/:path_id", learningHandler.GetEnrollmentDetail)
193		learnSelf.PUT("/progress", learningHandler.UpdateProgress)
194		learnSelf.GET("/progress", learningHandler.GetProgress)
195		learnSelf.GET("/export", learningHandler.ExportCSV)
196	
197		// ================================================================
198		// MODULE 3: Procurement & Disputes
199		// ProcSpec: Create/Read/Update orders+reviews. Approver: Read+Approve.
200		// ContentMod: Read appeals + arbitrate (hide/show/disclaimer).
201		// Finance: Read settlement view. Admin: Full.
202		// Learner: No access.
203		// ================================================================
204	
205		// Procurement read (orders, invoices, vendors, billing rules)
206		procRead := e.Group("/api/procurement", authMW.RequireAuth(),
207			authMW.RequireRole("procurement_specialist", "approver", "finance_analyst", "system_admin"))
208		procRead.GET("/vendors", procHandler.GetVendors)
209		procRead.GET("/orders", procHandler.GetOrders)
210		procRead.GET("/orders/:id", procHandler.GetOrder)
211		procRead.GET("/invoices", procHandler.GetInvoices)
212		procRead.GET("/invoices/:id", procHandler.GetInvoice)
213		procRead.GET("/billing-rules", procHandler.GetBillingRules)
214	
215		// Procurement write: create reviews with ratings/text/images, merchant replies
216		procWrite := e.Group("/api/procurement", authMW.RequireAuth(),
217			authMW.RequireRole("procurement_specialist", "system_admin"))
218		procWrite.GET("/reviews", procHandler.GetReviews)
219		procWrite.POST("/reviews", procHandler.CreateReview)
220		procWrite.POST("/reviews/reply", procHandler.CreateMerchantReply)
221	
222		// Approval actions (approve orders, match invoices)
223		procApprove := e.Group("/api/procurement", authMW.RequireAuth(),
224			authMW.RequireRole("approver", "system_admin"))
225		procApprove.PUT("/orders/:id/approve", procHandler.ApproveOrder)
226		procApprove.POST("/invoices/match", procHandler.MatchInvoice)
227	
228		// Dispute read (procurement_specialist, content_moderator, approver, admin)
229		disputeRead := e.Group("/api/procurement", authMW.RequireAuth(),
230			authMW.RequireRole("procurement_specialist", "content_moderator", "approver", "system_admin"))
231		disputeRead.GET("/disputes", procHandler.GetDisputes)
232		disputeRead.GET("/disputes/:id", procHandler.GetDispute)
233	
234		// Dispute creation (procurement users create appeals with evidence)
235		disputeCreate := e.Group("/api/procurement", authMW.RequireAuth(),
236			authMW.RequireRole("procurement_specialist", "system_admin"))
237		disputeCreate.POST("/disputes", procHandler.CreateDispute)
238	
239		// Dispute arbitration transitions (content_moderator + admin)
240		disputeArbitrate := e.Group("/api/procurement", authMW.RequireAuth(),
241			authMW.RequireRole("content_moderator", "system_admin"))
242		disputeArbitrate.POST("/disputes/transition", procHandler.TransitionDispute)
243	
244		// ================================================================
245		// MODULE 4: Reconciliation & Finance
246		// Finance: Create AR/AP, Read all, Update settlement status.
247		// Approver: Read variances, Approve write-offs.
248		// ProcSpec: Read relevant order costs only.
249		// Learner/ContentMod: No access.
250		// ================================================================
251	
252		// Finance: read ledger, create AR/AP entries, create settlements, compare, export
253		finRead := e.Group("/api/procurement", authMW.RequireAuth(),
254			authMW.RequireRole("finance_analyst", "system_admin"))
255		finRead.GET("/ledger", procHandler.GetLedger)
256		finRead.POST("/ledger", procHandler.CreateLedgerEntry)
257		finRead.GET("/cost-allocation", procHandler.GetCostAllocation)
258		finRead.POST("/reconciliation/compare", procHandler.CompareStatement)
259		finRead.POST("/settlements", procHandler.CreateSettlement)
260		finRead.GET("/export/ledger", procHandler.ExportLedger)
261		finRead.GET("/export/settlements", procHandler.ExportSettlements)
262	
263		// Settlements: finance reads + manages, approver reads + approves
264		settleGroup := e.Group("/api/procurement", authMW.RequireAuth(),
265			authMW.RequireRole("finance_analyst", "approver", "system_admin"))
266		settleGroup.GET("/settlements", procHandler.GetSettlements)
267		settleGroup.POST("/settlements/transition", procHandler.TransitionSettlement)
268	
269		// Start server
270		port := os.Getenv("PORT")
271		if port == "" {
272			port = "8080"
273		}
274	
275		go func() {
276			addr := fmt.Sprintf(":%s", port)
277			log.Printf("Starting server on %s", addr)
278			if err := e.Start(addr); err != nil {
279				log.Printf("Server stopped: %v", err)
280			}
281		}()
282	
283		// Graceful shutdown
284		quit := make(chan os.Signal, 1)
285		signal.Notify(quit, syscall.SIGINT, syscall.SIGTERM)
286		<-quit
287	
288		log.Println("Shutting down server...")
289		shutdownCtx, shutdownCancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 10*time.Second)
290		defer shutdownCancel()
291		if err := e.Shutdown(shutdownCtx); err != nil {
292			log.Printf("Server forced shutdown: %v", err)
293		}
294		cancel()
295		log.Println("Server exited")
296	}

266	-- ============================================================
267	-- TAXONOMY: Hierarchical Job/Skill Tags
268	-- ============================================================
269	
270	CREATE TYPE taxonomy_type AS ENUM ('job', 'skill', 'category', 'topic');
271	CREATE TYPE synonym_status AS ENUM ('active', 'pending_review', 'rejected');
272	CREATE TYPE taxonomy_review_action AS ENUM ('approve', 'reject', 'merge');
273	
274	CREATE TABLE taxonomy_tags (
275	    id              SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
276	    name            VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
277	    slug            VARCHAR(200) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
278	    tag_type        taxonomy_type NOT NULL,
279	    parent_id       INT REFERENCES taxonomy_tags(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
280	    canonical_id    INT REFERENCES taxonomy_tags(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
281	    pinyin          VARCHAR(500),                   -- pre-computed pinyin for Chinese matching
282	    description     TEXT,
283	    is_canonical    BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
284	    level           INT DEFAULT 0,                  -- depth in hierarchy
285	    sort_order      INT DEFAULT 0,
286	    usage_count     INT DEFAULT 0,
287	    created_by      INT REFERENCES users(id),
288	    created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
289	    updated_at      TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
290	);
291	
292	CREATE INDEX idx_taxonomy_parent ON taxonomy_tags(parent_id);
293	CREATE INDEX idx_taxonomy_type ON taxonomy_tags(tag_type);
294	CREATE INDEX idx_taxonomy_canonical ON taxonomy_tags(canonical_id);
295	CREATE INDEX idx_taxonomy_slug ON taxonomy_tags(slug);
296	CREATE INDEX idx_taxonomy_name_trgm ON taxonomy_tags USING gin (name gin_trgm_ops);
297	CREATE INDEX idx_taxonomy_pinyin_trgm ON taxonomy_tags USING gin (pinyin gin_trgm_ops);
298	
299	CREATE TABLE taxonomy_synonyms (
300	    id              SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
301	    term            VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
302	    canonical_tag_id INT NOT NULL REFERENCES taxonomy_tags(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
303	    status          synonym_status DEFAULT 'active',
304	    created_by      INT REFERENCES users(id),
305	    reviewed_by     INT REFERENCES users(id),
306	    reviewed_at     TIMESTAMPTZ,
307	    created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
308	    UNIQUE(term, canonical_tag_id)
309	);
310	
311	CREATE INDEX idx_synonyms_term ON taxonomy_synonyms(term);
312	CREATE INDEX idx_synonyms_term_trgm ON taxonomy_synonyms USING gin (term gin_trgm_ops);
313	CREATE INDEX idx_synonyms_canonical ON taxonomy_synonyms(canonical_tag_id);
314	CREATE INDEX idx_synonyms_status ON taxonomy_synonyms(status);
315	
316	-- Taxonomy review queue for admin approval
317	CREATE TABLE taxonomy_review_queue (
318	    id              SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
319	    entity_type     VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,           -- 'tag' or 'synonym'
320	    entity_id       INT NOT NULL,
321	    action          taxonomy_review_action,
322	    reason          TEXT,
323	    submitted_by    INT REFERENCES users(id),
324	    reviewed_by     INT REFERENCES users(id),
325	    reviewed_at     TIMESTAMPTZ,
326	    status          VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'pending',
327	    created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
328	);
329	
330	-- ============================================================
331	-- Synonym Conflict Detection Trigger
332	-- Blocks two active synonyms from pointing to different canonical tags
333	-- ============================================================
334	
335	CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION check_synonym_conflict()
336	RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
337	DECLARE
338	    existing_canonical INT;
339	BEGIN
340	    IF NEW.status = 'active' THEN
341	        SELECT canonical_tag_id INTO existing_canonical
342	        FROM taxonomy_synonyms
343	        WHERE term = NEW.term
344	          AND status = 'active'
345	          AND canonical_tag_id != NEW.canonical_tag_id
346	          AND id != COALESCE(NEW.id, 0)
347	        LIMIT 1;
348	
349	        IF existing_canonical IS NOT NULL THEN
350	            RAISE EXCEPTION
351	                'Synonym conflict: term "%" already has an active mapping to canonical tag ID %. '
352	                'Deactivate the existing synonym first or use the same canonical tag.',
353	                NEW.term, existing_canonical;
354	        END IF;
355	    END IF;
356	    RETURN NEW;
357	END;
358	$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
359	
360	CREATE TRIGGER trg_synonym_conflict_check
361	    BEFORE INSERT OR UPDATE ON taxonomy_synonyms
362	    FOR EACH ROW
363	    EXECUTE FUNCTION check_synonym_conflict();

365	-- ============================================================
366	-- RESOURCES (Learning Library)
367	-- ============================================================
368	
369	CREATE TYPE resource_status AS ENUM ('draft', 'published', 'archived');
370	CREATE TYPE resource_type AS ENUM ('article', 'video', 'course', 'document', 'link', 'assessment');
371	
372	CREATE TABLE resources (
373	    id              SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
374	    title           VARCHAR(500) NOT NULL,
375	    slug            VARCHAR(500) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
376	    description     TEXT,
377	    content_body    TEXT,
378	    resource_type   resource_type NOT NULL DEFAULT 'article',
379	    status          resource_status DEFAULT 'draft',
380	    category_id     INT REFERENCES taxonomy_tags(id),
381	    author_id       INT REFERENCES users(id),
382	    duration_mins   INT,
383	    difficulty      VARCHAR(20),                    -- beginner, intermediate, advanced
384	    thumbnail_url   TEXT,
385	    external_url    TEXT,
386	    view_count      INT DEFAULT 0,
387	    completion_count INT DEFAULT 0,
388	    popularity_score FLOAT DEFAULT 0,
389	    pinyin_title    VARCHAR(1000),                  -- pre-computed pinyin for title
390	    content_hash    VARCHAR(64),                    -- SHA-256 for near-duplicate detection
391	    search_vector   TSVECTOR,                       -- full-text search vector
392	    published_at    TIMESTAMPTZ,
393	    archived_at     TIMESTAMPTZ,
394	    created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
395	    updated_at      TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
396	);
397	
398	CREATE INDEX idx_resources_status ON resources(status);
399	CREATE INDEX idx_resources_category ON resources(category_id);
400	CREATE INDEX idx_resources_author ON resources(author_id);
401	CREATE INDEX idx_resources_published ON resources(published_at);
402	CREATE INDEX idx_resources_search ON resources USING gin(search_vector);
403	CREATE INDEX idx_resources_title_trgm ON resources USING gin(title gin_trgm_ops);
404	CREATE INDEX idx_resources_content_hash ON resources(content_hash);
405	CREATE INDEX idx_resources_popularity ON resources(popularity_score DESC);
406	
407	-- Resource-Tag many-to-many
408	CREATE TABLE resource_tags (
409	    resource_id INT NOT NULL REFERENCES resources(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
410	    tag_id      INT NOT NULL REFERENCES taxonomy_tags(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
411	    PRIMARY KEY (resource_id, tag_id)
412	);
413	
414	CREATE INDEX idx_resource_tags_tag ON resource_tags(tag_id);
415	
416	-- ============================================================
417	-- Auto-update search_vector on resource insert/update
418	-- Combines title (weight A), description (weight B), content_body (weight C),
419	-- and pinyin_title (weight D) into tsvector
420	-- ============================================================
421	
422	CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION resources_search_vector_update()
423	RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
424	BEGIN
425	    NEW.search_vector :=
426	        setweight(to_tsvector('english', COALESCE(NEW.title, '')), 'A') ||
427	        setweight(to_tsvector('english', COALESCE(NEW.description, '')), 'B') ||
428	        setweight(to_tsvector('english', COALESCE(NEW.content_body, '')), 'C') ||
429	        setweight(to_tsvector('simple', COALESCE(NEW.pinyin_title, '')), 'D');
430	    RETURN NEW;
431	END;
432	$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
433	
434	CREATE TRIGGER trg_resources_search_vector
435	    BEFORE INSERT OR UPDATE OF title, description, content_body, pinyin_title
436	    ON resources
437	    FOR EACH ROW
438	    EXECUTE FUNCTION resources_search_vector_update();
439	
440	-- ============================================================
441	-- Archive pages view (auto-generated by month and tag)
442	-- ============================================================
443	
444	CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW IF NOT EXISTS resource_archive_monthly AS
445	SELECT
446	    date_trunc('month', published_at) AS archive_month,
447	    category_id,
448	    COUNT(*) AS resource_count,
449	    array_agg(id ORDER BY published_at DESC) AS resource_ids
450	FROM resources
451	WHERE status = 'published' AND published_at IS NOT NULL
452	GROUP BY date_trunc('month', published_at), category_id
453	ORDER BY archive_month DESC;
454	
455	CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW IF NOT EXISTS resource_archive_by_tag AS
456	SELECT
457	    rt.tag_id,
458	    t.name AS tag_name,
459	    date_trunc('month', r.published_at) AS archive_month,
460	    COUNT(*) AS resource_count,
461	    array_agg(r.id ORDER BY r.published_at DESC) AS resource_ids
462	FROM resources r
463	JOIN resource_tags rt ON rt.resource_id = r.id
464	JOIN taxonomy_tags t ON t.id = rt.tag_id
465	WHERE r.status = 'published' AND r.published_at IS NOT NULL
466	GROUP BY rt.tag_id, t.name, date_trunc('month', r.published_at)
467	ORDER BY archive_month DESC;

796	-- Dispute State Machine Trigger
797	CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION validate_dispute_transition()
798	RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
799	BEGIN
800	    IF TG_OP = 'UPDATE' AND OLD.status != NEW.status THEN
801	        IF NOT (
802	            (OLD.status = 'created'           AND NEW.status = 'evidence_uploaded') OR
803	            (OLD.status = 'evidence_uploaded'  AND NEW.status = 'under_review')     OR
804	            (OLD.status = 'under_review'       AND NEW.status = 'arbitration')      OR
805	            (OLD.status = 'arbitration'        AND NEW.status IN ('resolved_hidden', 'resolved_disclaimer', 'resolved_restored', 'rejected'))
806	        ) THEN
807	            RAISE EXCEPTION 'Invalid dispute transition from % to %', OLD.status, NEW.status;
808	        END IF;
809	    END IF;
810	    RETURN NEW;
811	END;
812	$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
813	
814	CREATE TRIGGER trg_dispute_state_machine
815	    BEFORE UPDATE ON disputes
816	    FOR EACH ROW
817	    EXECUTE FUNCTION validate_dispute_transition();
818	
819	-- Invoice Variance Auto-Classify Trigger
820	CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION auto_classify_invoice_variance()
821	RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
822	DECLARE
823	    threshold NUMERIC(14,2);
824	BEGIN
825	    IF NEW.variance_amount IS NOT NULL AND NEW.status = 'variance_detected' THEN
826	        SELECT CAST(value AS NUMERIC) INTO threshold
827	        FROM configs WHERE key = 'variance.auto_writeoff_threshold';
828	        IF threshold IS NULL THEN threshold := 5.00; END IF;
829	        IF ABS(NEW.variance_amount) < threshold THEN
830	            NEW.status := 'pending_approval';
831	        ELSE
832	            NEW.status := 'manual_investigation';
833	        END IF;
834	    END IF;
835	    RETURN NEW;
836	END;
837	$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
838	
839	CREATE TRIGGER trg_invoice_variance_classify
840	    BEFORE INSERT OR UPDATE OF variance_amount, status ON invoices
841	    FOR EACH ROW
842	    EXECUTE FUNCTION auto_classify_invoice_variance();

106				&li.UnitPrice, &li.TotalPrice, &li.WarehouseCode, &li.TransportCode); err != nil {
107				return nil, err
108			}
109			items = append(items, li)
110		}
111		return items, rows.Err()
112	}
113	
114	func (r *ProcurementRepository) UpdateOrderStatus(ctx context.Context, id int, status string, approvedBy *int) error {
115		if approvedBy != nil {
116			_, err := r.db.Exec(ctx, `
117				UPDATE procurement_orders SET status = $2, approved_by = $3, approved_at = NOW(), updated_at = NOW()
118				WHERE id = $1
119			`, id, status, *approvedBy)
120			return err
121		}
122		_, err := r.db.Exec(ctx, `
123			UPDATE procurement_orders SET status = $2, updated_at = NOW() WHERE id = $1
124		`, id, status)
125		return err
126	}
127	
128	// ---- Invoices ----
129	
130	func (r *ProcurementRepository) GetInvoices(ctx context.Context, status string) ([]models.Invoice, error) {
131		query := `
132			SELECT i.id, i.invoice_number, i.order_id, i.vendor_id, v.name,
133				   i.status, i.invoice_amount, i.order_amount, i.variance_amount,
134				   i.variance_pct, i.currency, i.invoice_date::text, i.due_date::text,
135				   i.notes, i.matched_by, i.matched_at, i.created_at, i.updated_at
136			FROM invoices i
137			JOIN vendors v ON v.id = i.vendor_id
138		`
139		args := []interface{}{}
140		if status != "" {
141			query += " WHERE i.status = $1"
142			args = append(args, status)
143		}
144		query += " ORDER BY i.created_at DESC"
145	
146		rows, err := r.db.Query(ctx, query, args...)
147		if err != nil {
148			return nil, err
149		}
150		defer rows.Close()

1	package repository
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"strings"
7		"time"
8	
9		"wlpr-portal/internal/models"
10	
11		"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool"
12	)
13	
14	type SearchRepository struct {
15		db *pgxpool.Pool
16	}
17	
18	func NewSearchRepository(db *pgxpool.Pool) *SearchRepository {
19		return &SearchRepository{db: db}
20	}
21	
22	// SearchResources performs full-text search with tsvector ranking, pg_trgm fuzzy matching,
23	// optional synonym expansion, pinyin matching, multi-dimensional filtering, and sorting.
24	func (r *SearchRepository) SearchResources(ctx context.Context, req models.SearchRequest, synonymTagIDs []int, pinyinQuery string) (*models.SearchResponse, error) {
25		if req.Page < 1 {
26			req.Page = 1
27		}
28		if req.PageSize < 1 || req.PageSize > 100 {
29			req.PageSize = 20
30		}
31	
32		var conditions []string
33		var args []interface{}
34		argIdx := 1
35	
36		// Base: only published resources
37		conditions = append(conditions, "r.status = 'published'")
38	
39		// ---- Full-text & fuzzy search ----
40		hasQuery := strings.TrimSpace(req.Query) != ""
41		var tsQuery string
42		if hasQuery {
43			// Build tsquery from user input: split words and OR them with prefix matching
44			words := strings.Fields(req.Query)
45			tsTerms := make([]string, len(words))
46			for i, w := range words {
47				tsTerms[i] = w + ":*"
48			}
49			tsQuery = strings.Join(tsTerms, " | ")
50	
51			conditions = append(conditions, fmt.Sprintf(
52				"(r.search_vector @@ to_tsquery('english', $%d) OR similarity(r.title, $%d) > 0.15",
53				argIdx, argIdx+1,
54			))
55			args = append(args, tsQuery, req.Query)
56			argIdx += 2
57	
58			// Pinyin fuzzy matching
59			if pinyinQuery != "" {
60				conditions[len(conditions)-1] += fmt.Sprintf(
61					" OR similarity(COALESCE(r.pinyin_title,''), $%d) > 0.15", argIdx,
62				)
63				args = append(args, pinyinQuery)
64				argIdx++
65			}
66	
67			// Synonym-expanded tag matching
68			if len(synonymTagIDs) > 0 {
69				placeholders := make([]string, len(synonymTagIDs))
70				for i, id := range synonymTagIDs {
71					placeholders[i] = fmt.Sprintf("$%d", argIdx)
72					args = append(args, id)
73					argIdx++
74				}
75				conditions[len(conditions)-1] += fmt.Sprintf(
76					" OR r.id IN (SELECT resource_id FROM resource_tags WHERE tag_id IN (%s))",
77					strings.Join(placeholders, ","),
78				)
79			}
80	
81			conditions[len(conditions)-1] += ")" // close the big OR
82		}
83	
84		// ---- Multi-dimensional filters ----
85	
86		// Category filter
87		if len(req.Categories) > 0 {
88			placeholders := make([]string, len(req.Categories))
89			for i, cat := range req.Categories {
90				placeholders[i] = fmt.Sprintf("$%d", argIdx)
91				args = append(args, cat)
92				argIdx++
93			}
94			conditions = append(conditions, fmt.Sprintf(
95				"r.category_id IN (%s)", strings.Join(placeholders, ","),
96			))
97		}
98	
99		// Tag filter
100		if len(req.Tags) > 0 {
101			placeholders := make([]string, len(req.Tags))
102			for i, tag := range req.Tags {
103				placeholders[i] = fmt.Sprintf("$%d", argIdx)
104				args = append(args, tag)
105				argIdx++
106			}
107			conditions = append(conditions, fmt.Sprintf(
108				"r.id IN (SELECT resource_id FROM resource_tags WHERE tag_id IN (%s))",
109				strings.Join(placeholders, ","),
110			))
111		}
112	
113		// Date range
114		if req.DateFrom != "" {
115			if _, err := time.Parse("2006-01-02", req.DateFrom); err == nil {
116				conditions = append(conditions, fmt.Sprintf("r.published_at >= $%d", argIdx))
117				args = append(args, req.DateFrom)
118				argIdx++
119			}
120		}
121		if req.DateTo != "" {
122			if _, err := time.Parse("2006-01-02", req.DateTo); err == nil {
123				conditions = append(conditions, fmt.Sprintf("r.published_at <= $%d::date + INTERVAL '1 day'", argIdx))
124				args = append(args, req.DateTo)
125				argIdx++
126			}
127		}
128	
129		// Difficulty filter
130		if req.Difficulty != "" {
131			conditions = append(conditions, fmt.Sprintf("r.difficulty = $%d", argIdx))
132			args = append(args, req.Difficulty)
133			argIdx++
134		}
135	
136		// Resource type filter
137		if req.Type != "" {
138			conditions = append(conditions, fmt.Sprintf("r.resource_type = $%d", argIdx))
139			args = append(args, req.Type)
140			argIdx++
141		}
142	
143		whereClause := strings.Join(conditions, " AND ")
144	
145		// ---- Sorting ----
146		var orderClause string
147		switch req.SortBy {
148		case "popularity":
149			orderClause = "r.popularity_score DESC, r.view_count DESC"
150		case "recent":
151			orderClause = "r.published_at DESC NULLS LAST"
152		case "relevance":
153			if hasQuery {
154				orderClause = "search_rank DESC, trgm_sim DESC, r.popularity_score DESC"
155			} else {
156				orderClause = "r.popularity_score DESC"
157			}
158		default:
159			if hasQuery {
160				orderClause = "search_rank DESC, trgm_sim DESC, r.popularity_score DESC"
161			} else {
162				orderClause = "r.published_at DESC NULLS LAST"
163			}
164		}
165	
166		// ---- Build rank columns ----
167		var rankColumns string
168		if hasQuery {
169			rankColumns = fmt.Sprintf(`,
170				ts_rank_cd(r.search_vector, to_tsquery('english', $1)) AS search_rank,
171				GREATEST(
172					similarity(r.title, $2)%s
173				) AS trgm_sim`,
174				func() string {
175					if pinyinQuery != "" {
176						return fmt.Sprintf(", similarity(COALESCE(r.pinyin_title,''), $3)")
177					}
178					return ""
179				}(),
180			)
181		} else {
182			rankColumns = ", 0::float AS search_rank, 0::float AS trgm_sim"
183		}
184	
185		// ---- Count query ----
186		countQuery := fmt.Sprintf("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM resources r WHERE %s", whereClause)
187		var total int
188		if err := r.db.QueryRow(ctx, countQuery, args...).Scan(&total); err != nil {
189			return nil, fmt.Errorf("count query failed: %w", err)
190		}
191	
192		// ---- Main query ----
193		offset := (req.Page - 1) * req.PageSize
194	
195		mainQuery := fmt.Sprintf(`
196			SELECT r.id, r.title, r.slug, r.description, r.resource_type, r.status,
197				   r.category_id, COALESCE(cat.name, '') AS category_name,
198				   r.duration_mins, r.difficulty, r.thumbnail_url, r.external_url,
199				   r.view_count, r.completion_count, r.popularity_score,
200				   r.published_at, r.created_at, r.updated_at
201				   %s
202			FROM resources r
203			LEFT JOIN taxonomy_tags cat ON cat.id = r.category_id
204			WHERE %s
205			ORDER BY %s
206			LIMIT %d OFFSET %d
207		`, rankColumns, whereClause, orderClause, req.PageSize, offset)
208	
209		rows, err := r.db.Query(ctx, mainQuery, args...)
210		if err != nil {
211			return nil, fmt.Errorf("search query failed: %w", err)
212		}
213		defer rows.Close()
214	
215		results := make([]models.Resource, 0)
216		for rows.Next() {
217			var res models.Resource
218			var searchRank, trgmSim float64
219			if err := rows.Scan(
220				&res.ID, &res.Title, &res.Slug, &res.Description,
221				&res.ResourceType, &res.Status, &res.CategoryID, &res.CategoryName,
222				&res.DurationMins, &res.Difficulty, &res.ThumbnailURL, &res.ExternalURL,
223				&res.ViewCount, &res.CompletionCount, &res.PopularityScore,
224				&res.PublishedAt, &res.CreatedAt, &res.UpdatedAt,
225				&searchRank, &trgmSim,
226			); err != nil {
227				return nil, fmt.Errorf("scan error: %w", err)
228			}
229			res.SearchRank = searchRank
230			res.TrigramSimilarity = trgmSim
231			results = append(results, res)
232		}
233		if err := rows.Err(); err != nil {
234			return nil, err
235		}
236	
237		// Load tags for each result
238		for i := range results {
239			tags, err := r.GetResourceTags(ctx, results[i].ID)
240			if err == nil {
241				results[i].Tags = tags
242			}
243		}
244	
245		return &models.SearchResponse{
246			Results:  results,
247			Total:    total,
248			Page:     req.Page,
249			PageSize: req.PageSize,
250			Query:    req.Query,
251		}, nil
252	}
253	
254	func (r *SearchRepository) GetResourceTags(ctx context.Context, resourceID int) ([]models.TagBrief, error) {
255		rows, err := r.db.Query(ctx, `
256			SELECT t.id, t.name, t.slug
257			FROM taxonomy_tags t
258			JOIN resource_tags rt ON rt.tag_id = t.id
259			WHERE rt.resource_id = $1
260			ORDER BY t.name
261		`, resourceID)
262		if err != nil {
263			return nil, err
264		}
265		defer rows.Close()
266	
267		tags := make([]models.TagBrief, 0)
268		for rows.Next() {
269			var t models.TagBrief
270			if err := rows.Scan(&t.ID, &t.Name, &t.Slug); err != nil {
271				return nil, err
272			}
273			tags = append(tags, t)
274		}
275		return tags, rows.Err()
276	}
277	
278	func (r *SearchRepository) GetResourceByID(ctx context.Context, id int) (*models.Resource, error) {
279		res := &models.Resource{}
280		err := r.db.QueryRow(ctx, `
281			SELECT r.id, r.title, r.slug, r.description, r.content_body,
282				   r.resource_type, r.status, r.category_id,
283				   COALESCE(cat.name, '') AS category_name,
284				   r.author_id, r.duration_mins, r.difficulty,
285				   r.thumbnail_url, r.external_url,
286				   r.view_count, r.completion_count, r.popularity_score,
287				   r.pinyin_title, r.content_hash,
288				   r.published_at, r.created_at, r.updated_at
289			FROM resources r
290			LEFT JOIN taxonomy_tags cat ON cat.id = r.category_id
291			WHERE r.id = $1
292		`, id).Scan(
293			&res.ID, &res.Title, &res.Slug, &res.Description, &res.ContentBody,
294			&res.ResourceType, &res.Status, &res.CategoryID, &res.CategoryName,
295			&res.AuthorID, &res.DurationMins, &res.Difficulty,
296			&res.ThumbnailURL, &res.ExternalURL,
297			&res.ViewCount, &res.CompletionCount, &res.PopularityScore,
298			&res.PinyinTitle, &res.ContentHash,
299			&res.PublishedAt, &res.CreatedAt, &res.UpdatedAt,
300		)
301		if err != nil {
302			return nil, err
303		}
304	
305		tags, _ := r.GetResourceTags(ctx, res.ID)
306		res.Tags = tags
307		return res, nil
308	}
309	
310	func (r *SearchRepository) IncrementViewCount(ctx context.Context, resourceID int) error {
311		_, err := r.db.Exec(ctx, `
312			UPDATE resources SET view_count = view_count + 1, updated_at = NOW()
313			WHERE id = $1
314		`, resourceID)
315		return err
316	}
317	
318	func (r *SearchRepository) GetArchiveMonthly(ctx context.Context) ([]models.ArchivePage, error) {
319		// Refresh materialized view first
320		_, _ = r.db.Exec(ctx, "REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW resource_archive_monthly")
321	
322		rows, err := r.db.Query(ctx, `
323			SELECT to_char(a.archive_month, 'YYYY-MM') AS month,
324				   a.category_id,
325				   COALESCE(t.name, 'Uncategorized') AS category_name,
326				   a.resource_count
327			FROM resource_archive_monthly a
328			LEFT JOIN taxonomy_tags t ON t.id = a.category_id
329			ORDER BY a.archive_month DESC
330		`)
331		if err != nil {
332			return nil, err
333		}
334		defer rows.Close()
335	
336		pages := make([]models.ArchivePage, 0)
337		for rows.Next() {
338			var p models.ArchivePage
339			if err := rows.Scan(&p.Month, &p.CategoryID, &p.CategoryName, &p.ResourceCount); err != nil {
340				return nil, err
341			}
342			pages = append(pages, p)
343		}
344		return pages, rows.Err()
345	}
346	
347	// RecordEvent inserts a resource interaction event used by the recommendation engine.
348	func (r *SearchRepository) RecordEvent(ctx context.Context, userID, resourceID int, eventType string) error {
349		_, err := r.db.Exec(ctx, `
350			INSERT INTO resource_events (user_id, resource_id, event_type)
351			VALUES ($1, $2, $3)
352		`, userID, resourceID, eventType)
353		return err
354	}
355	
356	// RefreshArchiveViews refreshes both monthly and by-tag materialized views.
357	func (r *SearchRepository) RefreshArchiveViews(ctx context.Context) (int, error) {
358		_, err := r.db.Exec(ctx, "REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW resource_archive_monthly")
359		if err != nil {
360			return 0, fmt.Errorf("monthly archive refresh: %w", err)
361		}
362		_, err = r.db.Exec(ctx, "REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW resource_archive_by_tag")
363		if err != nil {
364			return 0, fmt.Errorf("tag archive refresh: %w", err)
365		}
366		return 1, nil
367	}

164	
165	func (r *LearningRepository) GetUserProgress(ctx context.Context, userID int, pathID *int) ([]models.UserProgress, error) {
166		query := `
167			SELECT up.id, up.user_id, up.resource_id, up.path_id, up.status,
168				   up.progress_pct, up.time_spent_mins, up.last_position,
169				   up.started_at, up.completed_at, up.synced_at,
170				   res.id, res.title, res.slug, res.resource_type,
171				   res.duration_mins, res.difficulty
172			FROM user_progress up
173			JOIN resources res ON res.id = up.resource_id
174			WHERE up.user_id = $1
175		`
176		args := []interface{}{userID}

208	func (r *LearningRepository) UpsertProgress(ctx context.Context, userID int, req models.UpdateProgressRequest) (*models.UserProgress, error) {
209		now := time.Now()
210		p := &models.UserProgress{}
211	
212		var startedAt *time.Time
213		if req.Status == "in_progress" || req.Status == "completed" {
214			startedAt = &now
215		}
216		var completedAt *time.Time
217		if req.Status == "completed" {
218			completedAt = &now
219		}
220	
221		err := r.db.QueryRow(ctx, `
222			INSERT INTO user_progress (user_id, resource_id, path_id, status, progress_pct,
223				time_spent_mins, last_position, started_at, completed_at, synced_at, updated_at)
224			VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5, $6, $7, $8, $9, NOW(), NOW())
225			ON CONFLICT (user_id, resource_id, path_id) DO UPDATE SET
226				status = EXCLUDED.status,
227				progress_pct = EXCLUDED.progress_pct,
228				time_spent_mins = user_progress.time_spent_mins + EXCLUDED.time_spent_mins,
229				last_position = COALESCE(EXCLUDED.last_position, user_progress.last_position),
230				started_at = COALESCE(user_progress.started_at, EXCLUDED.started_at),
231				completed_at = COALESCE(EXCLUDED.completed_at, user_progress.completed_at),
232				synced_at = NOW(),
233				updated_at = NOW()
234			RETURNING id, user_id, resource_id, path_id, status, progress_pct,
235					  time_spent_mins, last_position, started_at, completed_at, synced_at
236		`, userID, req.ResourceID, req.PathID, req.Status, req.ProgressPct,
237			req.TimeSpentMin, req.LastPosition, startedAt, completedAt,
238		).Scan(
239			&p.ID, &p.UserID, &p.ResourceID, &p.PathID, &p.Status,
240			&p.ProgressPct, &p.TimeSpentMin, &p.LastPosition,
241			&p.StartedAt, &p.CompletedAt, &p.SyncedAt,
242		)
243		return p, err
244	}

190	func (r *ProcurementRepository) MatchInvoice(ctx context.Context, invoiceID, orderID, matchedBy int) error {
191		_, err := r.db.Exec(ctx, `
192			UPDATE invoices SET
193				order_id = $2,
194				order_amount = (SELECT total_amount FROM procurement_orders WHERE id = $2),
195				variance_amount = invoice_amount - (SELECT total_amount FROM procurement_orders WHERE id = $2),
196				variance_pct = CASE
197					WHEN (SELECT total_amount FROM procurement_orders WHERE id = $2) = 0 THEN 0
198					ELSE ROUND(((invoice_amount - (SELECT total_amount FROM procurement_orders WHERE id = $2))
199						/ (SELECT total_amount FROM procurement_orders WHERE id = $2)) * 100, 2)
200				END,
201				status = CASE
202					WHEN invoice_amount = (SELECT total_amount FROM procurement_orders WHERE id = $2)
203					THEN 'matched'
204					ELSE 'variance_detected'
205				END,
206				matched_by = $3,
207				matched_at = NOW(),
208				updated_at = NOW()
209			WHERE id = $1
210		`, invoiceID, orderID, matchedBy)
211		return err
212	}

284	func (r *ProcurementRepository) SumLedgerByVendor(ctx context.Context, vendorID int, entryType, periodStart, periodEnd string) (float64, error) {
285		query := `

287			FROM ledger_entries
288			WHERE vendor_id = $1 AND entry_type = $2
289		`
290		args := []interface{}{vendorID, entryType}
291		idx := 3
292		if periodStart != "" {
293			query += fmt.Sprintf(" AND posted_at >= $%d", idx)
294			args = append(args, periodStart)
295			idx++
296		}
297		if periodEnd != "" {
298			query += fmt.Sprintf(" AND posted_at <= $%d::date + INTERVAL '1 day'", idx)
299			args = append(args, periodEnd)
300			idx++
301		}
302	
303		var total float64
304		err := r.db.QueryRow(ctx, query, args...).Scan(&total)
305		return total, err
306	}

17	// RecommendationWorker runs as a background goroutine, computing user-item
18	// similarity matrices and writing recommendations to the DB. It enforces the
19	// diversity control (no >40% from a single category) and job-family cold-start defaults.
20	type RecommendationWorker struct {
21		learningRepo *repository.LearningRepository
22		searchRepo   *repository.SearchRepository
23		configSvc    *ConfigService
24		maxCatPct    float64 // e.g., 0.40
25	}
26	
27	func NewRecommendationWorker(learningRepo *repository.LearningRepository, searchRepo *repository.SearchRepository, configSvc *ConfigService) *RecommendationWorker {
28		return &RecommendationWorker{
29			learningRepo: learningRepo,
30			searchRepo:   searchRepo,
31			configSvc:    configSvc,
32			maxCatPct:    0.40,
33		}
34	}
35	
36	// Start launches the nightly recommendation rebuild loop.
37	// It runs immediately once, then repeats on the given interval.
38	func (w *RecommendationWorker) Start(ctx context.Context, interval time.Duration) {
39		go func() {
40			// Run once on startup
41			w.run(ctx)
42	
43			ticker := time.NewTicker(interval)
44			defer ticker.Stop()
45			for {
46				select {
47				case <-ctx.Done():
48					log.Println("[rec-worker] shutting down")
49					return
50				case <-ticker.C:
51					w.run(ctx)
52				}
53			}
54		}()
55		log.Printf("[rec-worker] started with interval %s", interval)
56	}
57	
58	func (w *RecommendationWorker) run(ctx context.Context) {
59		start := time.Now()
60		log.Println("[rec-worker] starting recommendation computation...")
61	
62		// Reload max category percentage from config
63		if val, ok := w.configSvc.GetConfig("recommendation.max_category_pct"); ok {
64			var pct float64
65			if _, err := fmt.Sscanf(val, "%f", &pct); err == nil && pct > 0 && pct <= 100 {
66				w.maxCatPct = pct / 100.0
67			}
68		}
69	
70		userIDs, err := w.learningRepo.GetAllUserIDs(ctx)
71		if err != nil {
72			log.Printf("[rec-worker] failed to get user IDs: %v", err)
73			return
74		}
75	
76		allResources, err := w.learningRepo.GetAllPublishedResources(ctx)
77		if err != nil {
78			log.Printf("[rec-worker] failed to get resources: %v", err)
79			return
80		}
81	
82		// Build a global user-item interaction matrix
83		userVectors := make(map[int]map[int]float64)
84		for _, uid := range userIDs {
85			vec, err := w.learningRepo.GetUserEventVector(ctx, uid)
86			if err != nil {
87				continue
88			}
89			userVectors[uid] = vec
90		}
91	
92		batchID := uuid.New().String()
93	
94		for _, userID := range userIDs {
95			recs := w.computeForUser(ctx, userID, userVectors, allResources)
96	
97			// Apply diversity control
98			recs = w.applyDiversityControl(recs)
99	
100			// Write recommendations
101			for _, rec := range recs {
102				err := w.learningRepo.UpsertRecommendation(ctx,

1	package middleware

3	import (
4		"log"
5		"time"

7		"github.com/labstack/echo/v4"
8	)
9	
10	// RequestLogger logs each request with method, path, status, and duration.
11	// Sensitive headers (Authorization) are never logged.
12	func RequestLogger() echo.MiddlewareFunc {
13		return func(next echo.HandlerFunc) echo.HandlerFunc {
14			return func(c echo.Context) error {
15				start := time.Now()
16				err := next(c)
17				duration := time.Since(start)
18	
19				status := c.Response().Status
20				if err != nil {
21					if he, ok := err.(*echo.HTTPError); ok {
22						status = he.Code
23					}
24				}
25	
26				log.Printf("[%s] %s %s - %d (%s) ip=%s",
27					c.Request().Method,
28					c.Request().URL.Path,
29					c.Request().URL.RawQuery,
30					status,
31					duration,
32					c.RealIP(),
33				)
34	
35				return err
36			}
37		}
38	}

### 4. DATABASE SCHEMA (init.sql)

**Key Tables & Enums:**

**Authentication & Authorization:**

- `roles` - Role definitions (system_admin, content_moderator, learner, procurement_specialist, approver, finance_analyst)
- `permissions` - Fine-grained permission codes
- `role_permissions` - Many-to-many mapping
- `user_roles` - User-to-role assignment
- `sessions` - JWT session tracking with idle/max lifetime

**Configuration:**
- `feature_flags` - Feature flag control with rollout strategies (all, role_based, percentage, disabled)
- `configs` - Key-value configuration pairs with types
- `app_versions` - Version compatibility tracking
- `scheduled_jobs` - Cron job definitions

**Audit & Logging:**
- `audit_log` - Change tracking with before/after JSONB values

**Taxonomy:**
- `taxonomy_tags` - Hierarchical job/skill/category/topic tags with pinyin support
- `taxonomy_synonyms` - Synonym mapping with conflict detection trigger
- `taxonomy_review_queue` - Tag/synonym approval workflow

**Learning (Module 1):**
- `resources` - Learning library items (articles, videos, courses, documents, links, assessments)
- `resource_tags` - Many-to-many resource-to-tag mapping
- `learning_paths` - Structured learning path definitions
- `learning_path_items` - Path composition with required/elective item types
- `user_enrollments` - User enrollment in paths with completion tracking
- `user_progress` - Progress tracking per resource with resume bookmarks
- `resource_events` - Interaction events (view, complete, bookmark, share) for recommendations
- `recommendations` - Pre-computed recommendations with diversity control
- Materialized views: `resource_archive_monthly`, `resource_archive_by_tag`

**Procurement (Module 3):**
- `vendors` - Vendor master data
- `procurement_orders` - Purchase orders with status state machine
- `order_line_items` - Order line item details
- `invoices` - Invoice matching with variance detection and auto-classification
- `vendor_reviews` - User ratings & reviews with status management
- `merchant_replies` - Vendor responses to reviews
- `disputes` - Review appeal workflow with state machine validation trigger

**Finance (Module 4):**
- `ledger_entries` - AR/AP ledger entries grouped by department/cost center
- `settlements` - Settlement records with variance tracking and write-off management
- `billing_rules` - Rate/charge rules by rule type

**Triggers & Validation:**
- `check_synonym_conflict` - Prevents two active synonyms from mapping to different canonical tags
- `validate_dispute_transition` - Enforces valid dispute state transitions
- `auto_classify_invoice_variance` - Auto-classifies invoices based on variance threshold
- `resources_search_vector_update` - Maintains full-text search index

### 5. BACKEND ENTRY POINT: cmd/api/main.go

**Initialization:**
1. Encryption key initialization
2. JWT secret initialization

5. Background recommendation worker (24-hour interval)

**Route Groups:**

| Route Prefix | Auth Required | Public | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `/api/health` | No | Yes | Health check endpoint |
| `/api/auth/login`, `/register`, `/mfa/verify` | No | Yes | Public auth endpoints |
| `/api/auth/*` (protected) | Yes | No | Logout, me, MFA setup/confirm/disable |
| `/api/admin/*` | Yes (system_admin) | No | User management, role assignment |
| `/api/config/*` | Yes (system_admin) | No | Configuration & feature flag management |
| `/api/flags/:key/check` | Yes | No | Feature flag check |
| `/api/search`, `/api/resources/:id`, `/api/archives` | Yes (learner+) | No | Catalog search & resources |
| `/api/taxonomy/*` | Yes | No | Tag hierarchy, synonyms, reviews |
| `/api/learning/*` | Yes (learner+) | No | Paths, enrollment, progress, export |
| `/api/procurement/*` | Yes (procure+) | No | Orders, invoices, vendors, reviews, disputes |
| `/api/procurement/*` (finance) | Yes (finance) | No | Ledger, settlements, cost allocation, export |

### 6. REPOSITORY FILES SUMMARY

**config_repo.go:** Config & feature flag CRUD
**learning_repo.go:** Learning paths, enrollments, progress, recommendations, CSV export
**procurement_repo.go:** Orders, invoices, vendors, reviews, disputes, ledger, settlements, billing rules
**search_repo.go:** Full-text search with tsvector, pg_trgm fuzzy matching, synonym expansion, archive views, event recording
**taxonomy_repo.go:** Tag CRUD, hierarchy loading, synonym management, conflict checking
**user_repo.go:** User CRUD, roles, permissions, session management, password hashing, MFA secret storage

---

### 7. SERVICES SUMMARY

**auth_service.go:**
- Login with credential validation & optional MFA challenge
- MFA verification with token issuance
- Session creation/validation with idle/max lifetime enforcement
- Logout with session revocation

**config_service.go:**
- In-memory cached config/flag access with background DB sync
- Feature flag evaluation with rollout strategy support (all, role_based, percentage)
- Config update propagation

**search_service.go:**
- Full-text search with synonym expansion & pinyin matching
- Resource detail retrieval
- Archive page generation
- Search event recording for recommendations

**learning_service.go:**
- Path retrieval and enrollment management
- Progress tracking with path completion validation
- CSV export of learning records
- Recommendation retrieval

**mfa_service.go:**
- TOTP secret generation with QR code provisioning URI
- TOTP validation with ±1 period skew tolerance
- Recovery code generation/storage (8 alphanumeric codes)
- MFA enable/disable

**reconciliation.go:**
- Statement comparison with variance calculation
- Auto write-off threshold evaluation
- Invoice matching with variance-based status classification
- Settlement state machine transitions with write-off approval workflow

**export_sink.go:**
- Ledger/settlement export to file-drop directories or LAN webhooks
- Retry logic with configurable max retries and delay
- CSV generation with compensation support

**recommendation_worker.go:**
- Background worker (24-hour nightly rebuild)
- Collaborative filtering-based recommendations
- Cold-start defaults (job family matching + popularity)
- Diversity control (max 40% from single category)
- Batch-based recommendation cleanup

---

### 8. HANDLERS SUMMARY

**auth.go:**
- POST `/api/auth/login` - Credential validation, MFA challenge generation
- POST `/api/auth/register` - First user gets system_admin; others get default role
- POST `/api/auth/mfa/verify` - MFA code validation & token issuance
- POST `/api/auth/logout` - Session revocation
- GET `/api/auth/me` - Current user info
- POST `/api/auth/mfa/setup` - TOTP setup with QR code
- POST `/api/auth/mfa/confirm` - MFA enablement with recovery codes
- POST `/api/auth/mfa/disable` - MFA removal
- GET/POST `/api/admin/users` - User listing and role assignment

**config.go:**
- GET/PUT `/api/config/:key` - Config management
- GET `/api/config/all` - List all configs
- GET/PUT `/api/config/flags/:key` - Feature flag management
- GET `/api/flags/:key/check` - Flag evaluation (authenticated users)

**search.go:**
- GET `/api/search` - Full-text search with filtering, sorting, pagination
- GET `/api/resources/:id` - Resource detail with view event recording
- GET `/api/archives` - Paginated archive views

**taxonomy.go:**
- GET `/api/taxonomy/tags` - Tag listing with optional type filter
- GET `/api/taxonomy/tags/hierarchy` - Hierarchical tag tree
- GET `/api/taxonomy/tags/:id` - Tag detail with children
- POST `/api/taxonomy/tags` - Tag creation
- GET `/api/taxonomy/synonyms/:tag_id` - Synonym listing
- POST `/api/taxonomy/synonyms` - Synonym creation with conflict validation

**learning.go:**
- GET `/api/learning/paths` - Path listing
- GET `/api/learning/paths/:id` - Path detail with items
- POST `/api/learning/enroll` - User enrollment
- DELETE `/api/learning/enroll/:path_id` - Drop enrollment
- GET `/api/learning/enrollments` - User's enrollments
- GET `/api/learning/enrollments/:path_id` - Enrollment detail with progress
- PUT `/api/learning/progress` - Progress update
- GET `/api/learning/progress` - User progress records
- GET `/api/learning/export` - CSV export of learning records
- GET `/api/learning/recommendations` - Personalized recommendations

**procurement.go:**
- Vendors: GET `/api/procurement/vendors`
- Orders: GET/PUT (approve) `/api/procurement/orders*`
- Invoices: GET/POST (match) `/api/procurement/invoices*`
- Reviews: GET/POST/POST (reply) `/api/procurement/reviews*`
- Disputes: GET/POST/POST (transition) `/api/procurement/disputes*`
- Ledger: GET/POST `/api/procurement/ledger*`
- Settlements: GET/POST/POST (transition) `/api/procurement/settlements*`
- Cost Allocation: GET `/api/procurement/cost-allocation`
- Billing Rules: GET `/api/procurement/billing-rules`
- Exports: GET `/api/procurement/export/ledger`, GET `/api/procurement/export/settlements`

---

### 9. MIDDLEWARE FILES

**auth_middleware.go:**
- `RequireAuth()` - JWT validation, session validation (idle/max lifetime), claims injection
- `RequireRole(roles...)` - Role-based access control (returns 404 for unauthorized to prevent feature discovery)
- `RequirePermission(perms...)` - Permission-based access control (returns 404 for unauthorized)
- `AppVersionCheck()` - Version compatibility with grace period read-only mode (configurable days)
- `compareVersions()` - Semantic version comparison

**logging.go:**
- `RequestLogger()` - Logs method, path, query, status, duration, IP address

---

### KEY ARCHITECTURAL HIGHLIGHTS:

1. **4-Module System:**
   - Module 1: Learning & Development (resources, paths, enrollment, recommendations)
   - Module 2: Catalog & Taxonomy (hierarchical tags, synonyms, search)
   - Module 3: Procurement & Disputes (orders, invoices, reviews, dispute workflow)
   - Module 4: Reconciliation & Finance (AR/AP ledger, settlements, write-offs)

2. **Search Capabilities:**
   - Full-text search (tsvector) with A/B/C/D weights
   - Trigram similarity (pg_trgm) fuzzy matching >0.15 threshold
   - Synonym expansion via taxonomy synonyms
   - Pinyin matching for Chinese characters
   - Multi-dimensional filtering (category, tags, date, difficulty, type)
   - Multiple sort options (relevance, popularity, recent)

3. **Recommendation Engine:**
   - Nightly batch processing (24-hour intervals)
   - Collaborative filtering + cold-start defaults
   - Diversity control (max 40% from single category, configurable)
   - Job family targeting for new users
   - Popularity-based fallback

4. **State Machines:**
   - **Order Status:** draft → submitted → approved → fulfilled/cancelled
   - **Invoice Status:** pending → variance_detected/matched → pending_approval/manual_investigation → approved/rejected → paid
   - **Dispute Status:** created → evidence_uploaded → under_review → arbitration → resolved_*/rejected
   - **Settlement Status:** open → matched → variance_pending → writeoff_suggested → writeoff_approved → settled

5. **Security:**
   - AES-256 encryption for MFA secrets & recovery codes
   - Password hashing with bcrypt
   - JWT with role/permission claims
   - Session tracking with idle/max lifetime
   - TOTP (RFC 6238) with ±1 period skew tolerance
   - Feature invisibility (404 for unauthorized) on role checks
   - Version-based read-only grace period

6. **Multi-Tenancy:**
   - Role-based access control (6 roles with 18 permission codes)
   - Department & cost center allocation for finance
   - Vendor-specific review/dispute management

7. **Offline Support:**
   - File-drop directory export for finance data
   - LAN webhook delivery with retry logic
   - CSV exports for learning records

8. **Configuration Management:**
   - In-memory cached configs with background DB sync
   - Dynamic feature flags with rollout strategies
   - Version compatibility windows with grace periods
   - Threshold-based auto-classification (invoice variance, write-offs)

---

This codebase is a comprehensive enterprise workforce management portal with tightly integrated learning, procurement, and financial reconciliation modules. All files are production-ready and well-structured with clear separation of concerns across repository, service, handler, and middleware layers.

- frontend/src/components/SearchFilters.jsx

**Store (State Management):**
- frontend/src/store/authStore.js
- frontend/src/store/configStore.js

**API Client:**
- frontend/src/api/client.js

**Main:**
- frontend/src/App.jsx
- frontend/src/main.jsx

**Tests:**
- frontend/src/test/setup.js
- frontend/src/test/ProgressBar.test.jsx
- frontend/src/test/LoadingSpinner.test.jsx
- frontend/src/test/Login.test.jsx
- frontend/src/test/authStore.test.js
- frontend/src/test/ProtectedRoute.test.jsx
- frontend/src/test/Register.test.jsx

---

### 4. DATABASE SCHEMA (init.sql)

**Key Tables & Enums:**

**Authentication & Authorization:**
- `users` - Core user data with MFA support
- `roles` - Role definitions (system_admin, content_moderator, learner, procurement_specialist, approver, finance_analyst)
- `permissions` - Fine-grained permission codes
- `role_permissions` - Many-to-many mapping
- `user_roles` - User-to-role assignment
- `sessions` - JWT session tracking with idle/max lifetime

**Configuration:**
- `feature_flags` - Feature flag control with rollout strategies (all, role_based, percentage, disabled)
- `configs` - Key-value configuration pairs with types
- `app_versions` - Version compatibility tracking
- `scheduled_jobs` - Cron job definitions

**Audit & Logging:**
- `audit_log` - Change tracking with before/after JSONB values

**Taxonomy:**
- `taxonomy_tags` - Hierarchical job/skill/category/topic tags with pinyin support
- `taxonomy_synonyms` - Synonym mapping with conflict detection trigger
- `taxonomy_review_queue` - Tag/synonym approval workflow

**Learning (Module 1):**
- `resources` - Learning library items (articles, videos, courses, documents, links, assessments)
- `resource_tags` - Many-to-many resource-to-tag mapping
- `learning_paths` - Structured learning path definitions
- `learning_path_items` - Path composition with required/elective item types
- `user_enrollments` - User enrollment in paths with completion tracking
- `user_progress` - Progress tracking per resource with resume bookmarks
- `resource_events` - Interaction events (view, complete, bookmark, share) for recommendations
- `recommendations` - Pre-computed recommendations with diversity control
- Materialized views: `resource_archive_monthly`, `resource_archive_by_tag`

**Procurement (Module 3):**
- `vendors` - Vendor master data
- `procurement_orders` - Purchase orders with status state machine
- `order_line_items` - Order line item details
- `invoices` - Invoice matching with variance detection and auto-classification
- `vendor_reviews` - User ratings & reviews with status management
- `merchant_replies` - Vendor responses to reviews
- `disputes` - Review appeal workflow with state machine validation trigger

**Finance (Module 4):**
- `ledger_entries` - AR/AP ledger entries grouped by department/cost center
- `settlements` - Settlement records with variance tracking and write-off management
- `billing_rules` - Rate/charge rules by rule type

**Triggers & Validation:**
- `check_synonym_conflict` - Prevents two active synonyms from mapping to different canonical tags
- `validate_dispute_transition` - Enforces valid dispute state transitions
- `auto_classify_invoice_variance` - Auto-classifies invoices based on variance threshold
- `resources_search_vector_update` - Maintains full-text search index

---

### 5. BACKEND ENTRY POINT: cmd/api/main.go

**Initialization:**
1. Encryption key initialization
2. JWT secret initialization
3. PostgreSQL connection via pgxpool
4. Configuration service with background sync (30 seconds)
5. Background recommendation worker (24-hour interval)

**Route Groups:**

| Route Prefix | Auth Required | Public | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `/api/health` | No | Yes | Health check endpoint |
| `/api/auth/login`, `/register`, `/mfa/verify` | No | Yes | Public auth endpoints |
| `/api/auth/*` (protected) | Yes | No | Logout, me, MFA setup/confirm/disable |
| `/api/admin/*` | Yes (system_admin) | No | User management, role assignment |
| `/api/config/*` | Yes (system_admin) | No | Configuration & feature flag management |
| `/api/flags/:key/check` | Yes | No | Feature flag check |
| `/api/search`, `/api/resources/:id`, `/api/archives` | Yes (learner+) | No | Catalog search & resources |
| `/api/taxonomy/*` | Yes | No | Tag hierarchy, synonyms, reviews |
| `/api/learning/*` | Yes (learner+) | No | Paths, enrollment, progress, export |
| `/api/procurement/*` | Yes (procure+) | No | Orders, invoices, vendors, reviews, disputes |
| `/api/procurement/*` (finance) | Yes (finance) | No | Ledger, settlements, cost allocation, export |

---

### 6. REPOSITORY FILES SUMMARY

**config_repo.go:** Config & feature flag CRUD
**learning_repo.go:** Learning paths, enrollments, progress, recommendations, CSV export
**procurement_repo.go:** Orders, invoices, vendors, reviews, disputes, ledger, settlements, billing rules
**search_repo.go:** Full-text search with tsvector, pg_trgm fuzzy matching, synonym expansion, archive views, event recording
**taxonomy_repo.go:** Tag CRUD, hierarchy loading, synonym management, conflict checking
**user_repo.go:** User CRUD, roles, permissions, session management, password hashing, MFA secret storage

---

### 7. SERVICES SUMMARY

**auth_service.go:**
- Login with credential validation & optional MFA challenge
- MFA verification with token issuance
- Session creation/validation with idle/max lifetime enforcement
- Logout with session revocation

**config_service.go:**
- In-memory cached config/flag access with background DB sync
- Feature flag evaluation with rollout strategy support (all, role_based, percentage)
- Config update propagation

**search_service.go:**
- Full-text search with synonym expansion & pinyin matching
- Resource detail retrieval
- Archive page generation
- Search event recording for recommendations

**learning_service.go:**
- Path retrieval and enrollment management
- Progress tracking with path completion validation
- CSV export of learning records
- Recommendation retrieval

**mfa_service.go:**
- TOTP secret generation with QR code provisioning URI
- TOTP validation with ±1 period skew tolerance
- Recovery code generation/storage (8 alphanumeric codes)
- MFA enable/disable

**reconciliation.go:**
- Statement comparison with variance calculation
- Auto write-off threshold evaluation
- Invoice matching with variance-based status classification
- Settlement state machine transitions with write-off approval workflow

**export_sink.go:**
- Ledger/settlement export to file-drop directories or LAN webhooks
- Retry logic with configurable max retries and delay
- CSV generation with compensation support

**recommendation_worker.go:**
- Background worker (24-hour nightly rebuild)
- Collaborative filtering-based recommendations
- Cold-start defaults (job family matching + popularity)
- Diversity control (max 40% from single category)
- Batch-based recommendation cleanup

---

### 8. HANDLERS SUMMARY

**auth.go:**
- POST `/api/auth/login` - Credential validation, MFA challenge generation
- POST `/api/auth/register` - First user gets system_admin; others get default role
- POST `/api/auth/mfa/verify` - MFA code validation & token issuance
- POST `/api/auth/logout` - Session revocation
- GET `/api/auth/me` - Current user info
- POST `/api/auth/mfa/setup` - TOTP setup with QR code
- POST `/api/auth/mfa/confirm` - MFA enablement with recovery codes
- POST `/api/auth/mfa/disable` - MFA removal
- GET/POST `/api/admin/users` - User listing and role assignment

**config.go:**
- GET/PUT `/api/config/:key` - Config management
- GET `/api/config/all` - List all configs
- GET/PUT `/api/config/flags/:key` - Feature flag management
- GET `/api/flags/:key/check` - Flag evaluation (authenticated users)

**search.go:**
- GET `/api/search` - Full-text search with filtering, sorting, pagination
- GET `/api/resources/:id` - Resource detail with view event recording
- GET `/api/archives` - Paginated archive views

**taxonomy.go:**
- GET `/api/taxonomy/tags` - Tag listing with optional type filter
- GET `/api/taxonomy/tags/hierarchy` - Hierarchical tag tree
- GET `/api/taxonomy/tags/:id` - Tag detail with children
- POST `/api/taxonomy/tags` - Tag creation
- GET `/api/taxonomy/synonyms/:tag_id` - Synonym listing
- POST `/api/taxonomy/synonyms` - Synonym creation with conflict validation

**learning.go:**
- GET `/api/learning/paths` - Path listing
- GET `/api/learning/paths/:id` - Path detail with items
- POST `/api/learning/enroll` - User enrollment
- DELETE `/api/learning/enroll/:path_id` - Drop enrollment
- GET `/api/learning/enrollments` - User's enrollments
- GET `/api/learning/enrollments/:path_id` - Enrollment detail with progress
- PUT `/api/learning/progress` - Progress update
- GET `/api/learning/progress` - User progress records
- GET `/api/learning/export` - CSV export of learning records
- GET `/api/learning/recommendations` - Personalized recommendations

**procurement.go:**
- Vendors: GET `/api/procurement/vendors`
- Orders: GET/PUT (approve) `/api/procurement/orders*`
- Invoices: GET/POST (match) `/api/procurement/invoices*`
- Reviews: GET/POST/POST (reply) `/api/procurement/reviews*`
- Disputes: GET/POST/POST (transition) `/api/procurement/disputes*`
- Ledger: GET/POST `/api/procurement/ledger*`
- Settlements: GET/POST/POST (transition) `/api/procurement/settlements*`
- Cost Allocation: GET `/api/procurement/cost-allocation`
- Billing Rules: GET `/api/procurement/billing-rules`
- Exports: GET `/api/procurement/export/ledger`, GET `/api/procurement/export/settlements`

---

### 9. MIDDLEWARE FILES

**auth_middleware.go:**
- `RequireAuth()` - JWT validation, session validation (idle/max lifetime), claims injection
- `RequireRole(roles...)` - Role-based access control (returns 404 for unauthorized to prevent feature discovery)
- `RequirePermission(perms...)` - Permission-based access control (returns 404 for unauthorized)
- `AppVersionCheck()` - Version compatibility with grace period read-only mode (configurable days)
- `compareVersions()` - Semantic version comparison

**logging.go:**
- `RequestLogger()` - Logs method, path, query, status, duration, IP address

---

### KEY ARCHITECTURAL HIGHLIGHTS:

1. **4-Module System:**
   - Module 1: Learning & Development (resources, paths, enrollment, recommendations)
   - Module 2: Catalog & Taxonomy (hierarchical tags, synonyms, search)
   - Module 3: Procurement & Disputes (orders, invoices, reviews, dispute workflow)
   - Module 4: Reconciliation & Finance (AR/AP ledger, settlements, write-offs)

2. **Search Capabilities:**
   - Full-text search (tsvector) with A/B/C/D weights
   - Trigram similarity (pg_trgm) fuzzy matching >0.15 threshold
   - Synonym expansion via taxonomy synonyms
   - Pinyin matching for Chinese characters
   - Multi-dimensional filtering (category, tags, date, difficulty, type)
   - Multiple sort options (relevance, popularity, recent)

3. **Recommendation Engine:**
   - Nightly batch processing (24-hour intervals)
   - Collaborative filtering + cold-start defaults
   - Diversity control (max 40% from single category, configurable)
   - Job family targeting for new users
   - Popularity-based fallback

4. **State Machines:**
   - **Order Status:** draft → submitted → approved → fulfilled/cancelled
   - **Invoice Status:** pending → variance_detected/matched → pending_approval/manual_investigation → approved/rejected → paid
   - **Dispute Status:** created → evidence_uploaded → under_review → arbitration → resolved_*/rejected
   - **Settlement Status:** open → matched → variance_pending → writeoff_suggested → writeoff_approved → settled

5. **Security:**
   - AES-256 encryption for MFA secrets & recovery codes
   - Password hashing with bcrypt
   - JWT with role/permission claims
   - Session tracking with idle/max lifetime
   - TOTP (RFC 6238) with ±1 period skew tolerance
   - Feature invisibility (404 for unauthorized) on role checks
   - Version-based read-only grace period

6. **Multi-Tenancy:**
   - Role-based access control (6 roles with 18 permission codes)
   - Department & cost center allocation for finance
   - Vendor-specific review/dispute management

7. **Offline Support:**
   - File-drop directory export for finance data
   - LAN webhook delivery with retry logic
   - CSV exports for learning records

8. **Configuration Management:**
   - In-memory cached configs with background DB sync
   - Dynamic feature flags with rollout strategies
   - Version compatibility windows with grace periods
   - Threshold-based auto-classification (invoice variance, write-offs)

---

This codebase is a comprehensive enterprise workforce management portal with tightly integrated learning, procurement, and financial reconciliation modules. All files are production-ready and well-structured with clear separation of concerns across repository, service, handler, and middleware layers.

22	// SearchResources performs full-text search with tsvector ranking, pg_trgm fuzzy matching,
23	// optional synonym expansion, pinyin matching, multi-dimensional filtering, and sorting.
24	func (r *SearchRepository) SearchResources(ctx context.Context, req models.SearchRequest, synonymTagIDs []int, pinyinQuery string) (*models.SearchResponse, error) {
25		if req.Page < 1 {
26			req.Page = 1
27		}
28		if req.PageSize < 1 || req.PageSize > 100 {
29			req.PageSize = 20
30		}
31	
32		var conditions []string
33		var args []interface{}
34		argIdx := 1
35	
36		// Base: only published resources
37		conditions = append(conditions, "r.status = 'published'")
38	
39		// ---- Full-text & fuzzy search ----
40		hasQuery := strings.TrimSpace(req.Query) != ""
41		var tsQuery string
42		if hasQuery {
43			// Build tsquery from user input: split words and OR them with prefix matching
44			words := strings.Fields(req.Query)
45			tsTerms := make([]string, len(words))
46			for i, w := range words {
47				tsTerms[i] = w + ":*"
48			}
49			tsQuery = strings.Join(tsTerms, " | ")
50	
51			conditions = append(conditions, fmt.Sprintf(
52				"(r.search_vector @@ to_tsquery('english', $%d) OR similarity(r.title, $%d) > 0.15",
53				argIdx, argIdx+1,
54			))
55			args = append(args, tsQuery, req.Query)
56			argIdx += 2
57	
58			// Pinyin fuzzy matching
59			if pinyinQuery != "" {
60				conditions[len(conditions)-1] += fmt.Sprintf(
61					" OR similarity(COALESCE(r.pinyin_title,''), $%d) > 0.15", argIdx,
62				)
63				args = append(args, pinyinQuery)
64				argIdx++
65			}
66	
67			// Synonym-expanded tag matching
68			if len(synonymTagIDs) > 0 {
69				placeholders := make([]string, len(synonymTagIDs))
70				for i, id := range synonymTagIDs {
71					placeholders[i] = fmt.Sprintf("$%d", argIdx)
72					args = append(args, id)
73					argIdx++
74				}
75				conditions[len(conditions)-1] += fmt.Sprintf(
76					" OR r.id IN (SELECT resource_id FROM resource_tags WHERE tag_id IN (%s))",
77					strings.Join(placeholders, ","),
78				)
79			}
80	
81			conditions[len(conditions)-1] += ")" // close the big OR
82		}
83	
84		// ---- Multi-dimensional filters ----
85	
86		// Category filter
87		if len(req.Categories) > 0 {
88			placeholders := make([]string, len(req.Categories))
89			for i, cat := range req.Categories {
90				placeholders[i] = fmt.Sprintf("$%d", argIdx)
91				args = append(args, cat)
92				argIdx++
93			}
94			conditions = append(conditions, fmt.Sprintf(
95				"r.category_id IN (%s)", strings.Join(placeholders, ","),
96			))
97		}
98	
99		// Tag filter
100		if len(req.Tags) > 0 {
101			placeholders := make([]string, len(req.Tags))
102			for i, tag := range req.Tags {
103				placeholders[i] = fmt.Sprintf("$%d", argIdx)
104				args = append(args, tag)
105				argIdx++
106			}
107			conditions = append(conditions, fmt.Sprintf(
108				"r.id IN (SELECT resource_id FROM resource_tags WHERE tag_id IN (%s))",
109				strings.Join(placeholders, ","),
110			))
111		}
112	
113		// Date range
114		if req.DateFrom != "" {
115			if _, err := time.Parse("2006-01-02", req.DateFrom); err == nil {
116				conditions = append(conditions, fmt.Sprintf("r.published_at >= $%d", argIdx))
117				args = append(args, req.DateFrom)
118				argIdx++
119			}
120		}
121		if req.DateTo != "" {
122			if _, err := time.Parse("2006-01-02", req.DateTo); err == nil {
123				conditions = append(conditions, fmt.Sprintf("r.published_at <= $%d::date + INTERVAL '1 day'", argIdx))
124				args = append(args, req.DateTo)
125				argIdx++
126			}
127		}
128	
129		// Difficulty filter
130		if req.Difficulty != "" {
131			conditions = append(conditions, fmt.Sprintf("r.difficulty = $%d", argIdx))
132			args = append(args, req.Difficulty)
133			argIdx++
134		}
135	
136		// Resource type filter
137		if req.Type != "" {
138			conditions = append(conditions, fmt.Sprintf("r.resource_type = $%d", argIdx))
139			args = append(args, req.Type)
140			argIdx++
141		}
142	
143		whereClause := strings.Join(conditions, " AND ")
144	
145		// ---- Sorting ----
146		var orderClause string
147		switch req.SortBy {
148		case "popularity":
149			orderClause = "r.popularity_score DESC, r.view_count DESC"
150		case "recent":
151			orderClause = "r.published_at DESC NULLS LAST"
152		case "relevance":
153			if hasQuery {
154				orderClause = "search_rank DESC, trgm_sim DESC, r.popularity_score DESC"
155			} else {
156				orderClause = "r.popularity_score DESC"
157			}
158		default:
159			if hasQuery {
160				orderClause = "search_rank DESC, trgm_sim DESC, r.popularity_score DESC"
161			} else {
162				orderClause = "r.published_at DESC NULLS LAST"
163			}
164		}
165	
166		// ---- Build rank columns ----
167		var rankColumns string
168		if hasQuery {
169			rankColumns = fmt.Sprintf(`,
170				ts_rank_cd(r.search_vector, to_tsquery('english', $1)) AS search_rank,
171				GREATEST(
172					similarity(r.title, $2)%s
173				) AS trgm_sim`,
174				func() string {
175					if pinyinQuery != "" {

177					}
