179				}(),
180			)
181		} else {
182			rankColumns = ", 0::float AS search_rank, 0::float AS trgm_sim"
183		}
184	
185		// ---- Count query ----
186		countQuery := fmt.Sprintf("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM resources r WHERE %s", whereClause)
187		var total int
188		if err := r.db.QueryRow(ctx, countQuery, args...).Scan(&total); err != nil {
189			return nil, fmt.Errorf("count query failed: %w", err)
190		}
191	
192		// ---- Main query ----
193		offset := (req.Page - 1) * req.PageSize
194	
195		mainQuery := fmt.Sprintf(`
196			SELECT r.id, r.title, r.slug, r.description, r.resource_type, r.status,
197				   r.category_id, COALESCE(cat.name, '') AS category_name,
198				   r.duration_mins, r.difficulty, r.thumbnail_url, r.external_url,
199				   r.view_count, r.completion_count, r.popularity_score,
200				   r.published_at, r.created_at, r.updated_at
201				   %s
202			FROM resources r
203			LEFT JOIN taxonomy_tags cat ON cat.id = r.category_id
204			WHERE %s
205			ORDER BY %s
206			LIMIT %d OFFSET %d
207		`, rankColumns, whereClause, orderClause, req.PageSize, offset)
208	
209		rows, err := r.db.Query(ctx, mainQuery, args...)
210		if err != nil {
211			return nil, fmt.Errorf("search query failed: %w", err)
212		}
213		defer rows.Close()
214	
215		results := make([]models.Resource, 0)
216		for rows.Next() {
217			var res models.Resource
218			var searchRank, trgmSim float64
219			if err := rows.Scan(
220				&res.ID, &res.Title, &res.Slug, &res.Description,
221				&res.ResourceType, &res.Status, &res.CategoryID, &res.CategoryName,
222				&res.DurationMins, &res.Difficulty, &res.ThumbnailURL, &res.ExternalURL,
223				&res.ViewCount, &res.CompletionCount, &res.PopularityScore,
224				&res.PublishedAt, &res.CreatedAt, &res.UpdatedAt,
225				&searchRank, &trgmSim,
226			); err != nil {
227				return nil, fmt.Errorf("scan error: %w", err)
228			}
229			res.SearchRank = searchRank
230			res.TrigramSimilarity = trgmSim
231			results = append(results, res)
232		}
233		if err := rows.Err(); err != nil {
234			return nil, err
235		}
236	
237		// Load tags for each result
238		for i := range results {
239			tags, err := r.GetResourceTags(ctx, results[i].ID)
240			if err == nil {
241				results[i].Tags = tags
242			}
243		}
244	
245		return &models.SearchResponse{
246			Results:  results,
247			Total:    total,
248			Page:     req.Page,
249			PageSize: req.PageSize,
250			Query:    req.Query,
251		}, nil
252	}

254	func (r *SearchRepository) GetResourceTags(ctx context.Context, resourceID int) ([]models.TagBrief, error) {
255		rows, err := r.db.Query(ctx, `
256			SELECT t.id, t.name, t.slug

259			WHERE rt.resource_id = $1
260			ORDER BY t.name
261		`, resourceID)
262		if err != nil {
263			return nil, err
264		}
265		defer rows.Close()
266

270			if err := rows.Scan(&t.ID, &t.Name, &t.Slug); err != nil {
271				return nil, err
272			}
273			tags = append(tags, t)
274		}
275		return tags, rows.Err()
276	}

318	func (r *SearchRepository) GetArchiveMonthly(ctx context.Context) ([]models.ArchivePage, error) {
319		// Refresh materialized view first
320		_, _ = r.db.Exec(ctx, "REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW resource_archive_monthly")
321	
322		rows, err := r.db.Query(ctx, `
323			SELECT to_char(a.archive_month, 'YYYY-MM') AS month,
324				   a.category_id,
325				   COALESCE(t.name, 'Uncategorized') AS category_name,
326				   a.resource_count
327			FROM resource_archive_monthly a
328			LEFT JOIN taxonomy_tags t ON t.id = a.category_id
329			ORDER BY a.archive_month DESC
330		`)
331		if err != nil {
332			return nil, err
333		}
334		defer rows.Close()
335	
336		pages := make([]models.ArchivePage, 0)
337		for rows.Next() {
338			var p models.ArchivePage
339			if err := rows.Scan(&p.Month, &p.CategoryID, &p.CategoryName, &p.ResourceCount); err != nil {
340				return nil, err
341			}
342			pages = append(pages, p)
343		}
344		return pages, rows.Err()
345	}
346	
347	// RecordEvent inserts a resource interaction event used by the recommendation engine.
348	func (r *SearchRepository) RecordEvent(ctx context.Context, userID, resourceID int, eventType string) error {
349		_, err := r.db.Exec(ctx, `
350			INSERT INTO resource_events (user_id, resource_id, event_type)
351			VALUES ($1, $2, $3)
352		`, userID, resourceID, eventType)
353		return err
354	}
355	
356	// RefreshArchiveViews refreshes both monthly and by-tag materialized views.
357	func (r *SearchRepository) RefreshArchiveViews(ctx context.Context) (int, error) {
358		_, err := r.db.Exec(ctx, "REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW resource_archive_monthly")
359		if err != nil {
360			return 0, fmt.Errorf("monthly archive refresh: %w", err)
361		}
362		_, err = r.db.Exec(ctx, "REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW resource_archive_by_tag")
363		if err != nil {
364			return 0, fmt.Errorf("tag archive refresh: %w", err)
365		}
366		return 1, nil
367	}

267	-- TAXONOMY: Hierarchical Job/Skill Tags

269	
270	CREATE TYPE taxonomy_type AS ENUM ('job', 'skill', 'category', 'topic');
271	CREATE TYPE synonym_status AS ENUM ('active', 'pending_review', 'rejected');
272	CREATE TYPE taxonomy_review_action AS ENUM ('approve', 'reject', 'merge');

274	CREATE TABLE taxonomy_tags (
275	    id              SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
276	    name            VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
277	    slug            VARCHAR(200) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
278	    tag_type        taxonomy_type NOT NULL,
279	    parent_id       INT REFERENCES taxonomy_tags(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
280	    canonical_id    INT REFERENCES taxonomy_tags(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
281	    pinyin          VARCHAR(500),                   -- pre-computed pinyin for Chinese matching
282	    description     TEXT,
283	    is_canonical    BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
284	    level           INT DEFAULT 0,                  -- depth in hierarchy
285	    sort_order      INT DEFAULT 0,
286	    usage_count     INT DEFAULT 0,
287	    created_by      INT REFERENCES users(id),
288	    created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
289	    updated_at      TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
290	);
291	
292	CREATE INDEX idx_taxonomy_parent ON taxonomy_tags(parent_id);
293	CREATE INDEX idx_taxonomy_type ON taxonomy_tags(tag_type);
294	CREATE INDEX idx_taxonomy_canonical ON taxonomy_tags(canonical_id);
295	CREATE INDEX idx_taxonomy_slug ON taxonomy_tags(slug);
296	CREATE INDEX idx_taxonomy_name_trgm ON taxonomy_tags USING gin (name gin_trgm_ops);
297	CREATE INDEX idx_taxonomy_pinyin_trgm ON taxonomy_tags USING gin (pinyin gin_trgm_ops);
298	
299	CREATE TABLE taxonomy_synonyms (
300	    id              SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
301	    term            VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
302	    canonical_tag_id INT NOT NULL REFERENCES taxonomy_tags(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
303	    status          synonym_status DEFAULT 'active',
304	    created_by      INT REFERENCES users(id),
305	    reviewed_by     INT REFERENCES users(id),
306	    reviewed_at     TIMESTAMPTZ,
307	    created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
308	    UNIQUE(term, canonical_tag_id)
309	);
310	
311	CREATE INDEX idx_synonyms_term ON taxonomy_synonyms(term);
312	CREATE INDEX idx_synonyms_term_trgm ON taxonomy_synonyms USING gin (term gin_trgm_ops);
313	CREATE INDEX idx_synonyms_canonical ON taxonomy_synonyms(canonical_tag_id);
314	CREATE INDEX idx_synonyms_status ON taxonomy_synonyms(status);
315	
316	-- Taxonomy review queue for admin approval
317	CREATE TABLE taxonomy_review_queue (
318	    id              SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
319	    entity_type     VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,           -- 'tag' or 'synonym'
320	    entity_id       INT NOT NULL,
321	    action          taxonomy_review_action,
322	    reason          TEXT,
323	    submitted_by    INT REFERENCES users(id),
324	    reviewed_by     INT REFERENCES users(id),
325	    reviewed_at     TIMESTAMPTZ,
326	    status          VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'pending',
327	    created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
328	);

330	-- ============================================================
331	-- Synonym Conflict Detection Trigger
332	-- Blocks two active synonyms from pointing to different canonical tags
333	-- ============================================================
334	
335	CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION check_synonym_conflict()
336	RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
337	DECLARE
338	    existing_canonical INT;
339	BEGIN
340	    IF NEW.status = 'active' THEN
341	        SELECT canonical_tag_id INTO existing_canonical
342	        FROM taxonomy_synonyms
343	        WHERE term = NEW.term
344	          AND status = 'active'
345	          AND canonical_tag_id != NEW.canonical_tag_id
346	          AND id != COALESCE(NEW.id, 0)
347	        LIMIT 1;
348	
349	        IF existing_canonical IS NOT NULL THEN
350	            RAISE EXCEPTION
351	                'Synonym conflict: term "%" already has an active mapping to canonical tag ID %. '
352	                'Deactivate the existing synonym first or use the same canonical tag.',
353	                NEW.term, existing_canonical;
354	        END IF;
355	    END IF;
356	    RETURN NEW;
357	END;
358	$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
359	
360	CREATE TRIGGER trg_synonym_conflict_check
361	    BEFORE INSERT OR UPDATE ON taxonomy_synonyms
362	    FOR EACH ROW
363	    EXECUTE FUNCTION check_synonym_conflict();

390	    content_hash    VARCHAR(64),                    -- SHA-256 for near-duplicate detection
391	    search_vector   TSVECTOR,                       -- full-text search vector

407	-- Resource-Tag many-to-many
408	CREATE TABLE resource_tags (
409	    resource_id INT NOT NULL REFERENCES resources(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
410	    tag_id      INT NOT NULL REFERENCES taxonomy_tags(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,

412	);

415	
416	-- ============================================================
417	-- Auto-update search_vector on resource insert/update
418	-- Combines title (weight A), description (weight B), content_body (weight C),
419	-- and pinyin_title (weight D) into tsvector
420	-- ============================================================
421	
422	CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION resources_search_vector_update()
423	RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
424	BEGIN
425	    NEW.search_vector :=
426	        setweight(to_tsvector('english', COALESCE(NEW.title, '')), 'A') ||
427	        setweight(to_tsvector('english', COALESCE(NEW.description, '')), 'B') ||
428	        setweight(to_tsvector('english', COALESCE(NEW.content_body, '')), 'C') ||
429	        setweight(to_tsvector('simple', COALESCE(NEW.pinyin_title, '')), 'D');
430	    RETURN NEW;
431	END;
432	$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
433	
434	CREATE TRIGGER trg_resources_search_vector
435	    BEFORE INSERT OR UPDATE OF title, description, content_body, pinyin_title
436	    ON resources
437	    FOR EACH ROW
438	    EXECUTE FUNCTION resources_search_vector_update();

440	-- ============================================================
441	-- Archive pages view (auto-generated by month and tag)
442	-- ============================================================
443	
444	CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW IF NOT EXISTS resource_archive_monthly AS
445	SELECT
446	    date_trunc('month', published_at) AS archive_month,
447	    category_id,
448	    COUNT(*) AS resource_count,
449	    array_agg(id ORDER BY published_at DESC) AS resource_ids
450	FROM resources
451	WHERE status = 'published' AND published_at IS NOT NULL
452	GROUP BY date_trunc('month', published_at), category_id
453	ORDER BY archive_month DESC;
454	
455	CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW IF NOT EXISTS resource_archive_by_tag AS
456	SELECT
457	    rt.tag_id,
458	    t.name AS tag_name,
459	    date_trunc('month', r.published_at) AS archive_month,
460	    COUNT(*) AS resource_count,
461	    array_agg(r.id ORDER BY r.published_at DESC) AS resource_ids
462	FROM resources r
463	JOIN resource_tags rt ON rt.resource_id = r.id
464	JOIN taxonomy_tags t ON t.id = rt.tag_id
465	WHERE r.status = 'published' AND r.published_at IS NOT NULL
466	GROUP BY rt.tag_id, t.name, date_trunc('month', r.published_at)
467	ORDER BY archive_month DESC;

712	    approved_at     TIMESTAMPTZ,
713	    settled_at      TIMESTAMPTZ,
714	    created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
715	    updated_at      TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
716	);
717	
718	CREATE INDEX idx_settlements_vendor ON settlements(vendor_id);
719	CREATE INDEX idx_settlements_status ON settlements(status);
720	
721	CREATE TABLE billing_rules (
722	    id              SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
723	    rule_type       VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL,
724	    code            VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
725	    description     TEXT,
726	    rate_per_unit   NUMERIC(14,4) NOT NULL,
727	    min_charge      NUMERIC(14,2) DEFAULT 0,
728	    max_charge      NUMERIC(14,2),
729	    effective_from  DATE NOT NULL,
730	    effective_to    DATE,
731	    is_active       BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
732	    created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
733	    UNIQUE(rule_type, code, effective_from)
734	);
735	
736	-- ============================================================
737	-- REVIEWS & DISPUTES
738	-- ============================================================
739	
740	CREATE TYPE review_status AS ENUM ('visible', 'hidden', 'disclaimer');
741	CREATE TYPE dispute_status AS ENUM (
742	    'created', 'evidence_uploaded', 'under_review',
743	    'arbitration', 'resolved_hidden', 'resolved_disclaimer',
744	    'resolved_restored', 'rejected'
745	);
746	
747	CREATE TABLE vendor_reviews (
748	    id              SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
749	    vendor_id       INT NOT NULL REFERENCES vendors(id),
750	    order_id        INT REFERENCES procurement_orders(id),
751	    reviewer_id     INT NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
752	    rating          INT NOT NULL CHECK (rating BETWEEN 1 AND 5),
753	    title           VARCHAR(300),
754	    body            TEXT NOT NULL,
755	    image_urls      TEXT[],
756	    review_status   review_status DEFAULT 'visible',
757	    disclaimer_text TEXT,
758	    created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
759	    updated_at      TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
760	);
761	
762	CREATE INDEX idx_reviews_vendor ON vendor_reviews(vendor_id);
763	CREATE INDEX idx_reviews_status ON vendor_reviews(review_status);
764	
765	CREATE TABLE merchant_replies (
766	    id              SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
767	    review_id       INT NOT NULL REFERENCES vendor_reviews(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
768	    vendor_id       INT NOT NULL REFERENCES vendors(id),
769	    body            TEXT NOT NULL,
770	    replied_by      INT REFERENCES users(id),
771	    created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
772	);
773	
774	CREATE TABLE disputes (
775	    id                  SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
776	    review_id           INT NOT NULL REFERENCES vendor_reviews(id),
777	    vendor_id           INT NOT NULL REFERENCES vendors(id),
778	    status              dispute_status DEFAULT 'created',
779	    reason              TEXT NOT NULL,
780	    evidence_urls       TEXT[],
781	    evidence_metadata_enc BYTEA,
782	    merchant_response   TEXT,
783	    arbitration_notes   TEXT,
784	    arbitration_outcome VARCHAR(30),
785	    arbitrated_by       INT REFERENCES users(id),
786	    arbitrated_at       TIMESTAMPTZ,
787	    created_by          INT NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
788	    created_at          TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
789	    updated_at          TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
790	);
791	
792	CREATE INDEX idx_disputes_review ON disputes(review_id);
793	CREATE INDEX idx_disputes_vendor ON disputes(vendor_id);
794	CREATE INDEX idx_disputes_status ON disputes(status);
795	
796	-- Dispute State Machine Trigger
797	CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION validate_dispute_transition()
798	RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
799	BEGIN
800	    IF TG_OP = 'UPDATE' AND OLD.status != NEW.status THEN
801	        IF NOT (
802	            (OLD.status = 'created'           AND NEW.status = 'evidence_uploaded') OR
803	            (OLD.status = 'evidence_uploaded'  AND NEW.status = 'under_review')     OR
804	            (OLD.status = 'under_review'       AND NEW.status = 'arbitration')      OR
805	            (OLD.status = 'arbitration'        AND NEW.status IN ('resolved_hidden', 'resolved_disclaimer', 'resolved_restored', 'rejected'))
806	        ) THEN
807	            RAISE EXCEPTION 'Invalid dispute transition from % to %', OLD.status, NEW.status;
808	        END IF;
809	    END IF;
810	    RETURN NEW;
811	END;
812	$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
813	
814	CREATE TRIGGER trg_dispute_state_machine
815	    BEFORE UPDATE ON disputes
816	    FOR EACH ROW
817	    EXECUTE FUNCTION validate_dispute_transition();
818	
819	-- Invoice Variance Auto-Classify Trigger
820	CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION auto_classify_invoice_variance()
821	RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
822	DECLARE
823	    threshold NUMERIC(14,2);
824	BEGIN
825	    IF NEW.variance_amount IS NOT NULL AND NEW.status = 'variance_detected' THEN
826	        SELECT CAST(value AS NUMERIC) INTO threshold
827	        FROM configs WHERE key = 'variance.auto_writeoff_threshold';
828	        IF threshold IS NULL THEN threshold := 5.00; END IF;
829	        IF ABS(NEW.variance_amount) < threshold THEN
830	            NEW.status := 'pending_approval';
831	        ELSE
832	            NEW.status := 'manual_investigation';
833	        END IF;
834	    END IF;
835	    RETURN NEW;
836	END;
837	$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
838	
839	CREATE TRIGGER trg_invoice_variance_classify
840	    BEFORE INSERT OR UPDATE OF variance_amount, status ON invoices
841	    FOR EACH ROW
842	    EXECUTE FUNCTION auto_classify_invoice_variance();
843	
844	-- No seeded data: all content is created by users at runtime.
845

208	func (r *LearningRepository) UpsertProgress(ctx context.Context, userID int, req models.UpdateProgressRequest) (*models.UserProgress, error) {
209		now := time.Now()
210		p := &models.UserProgress{}
211	
212		var startedAt *time.Time
213		if req.Status == "in_progress" || req.Status == "completed" {
214			startedAt = &now
215		}
216		var completedAt *time.Time
217		if req.Status == "completed" {
218			completedAt = &now
219		}
220	
221		err := r.db.QueryRow(ctx, `
222			INSERT INTO user_progress (user_id, resource_id, path_id, status, progress_pct,
223				time_spent_mins, last_position, started_at, completed_at, synced_at, updated_at)
224			VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5, $6, $7, $8, $9, NOW(), NOW())
225			ON CONFLICT (user_id, resource_id, path_id) DO UPDATE SET
226				status = EXCLUDED.status,
227				progress_pct = EXCLUDED.progress_pct,
228				time_spent_mins = user_progress.time_spent_mins + EXCLUDED.time_spent_mins,
229				last_position = COALESCE(EXCLUDED.last_position, user_progress.last_position),
230				started_at = COALESCE(user_progress.started_at, EXCLUDED.started_at),
231				completed_at = COALESCE(EXCLUDED.completed_at, user_progress.completed_at),
232				synced_at = NOW(),
233				updated_at = NOW()
234			RETURNING id, user_id, resource_id, path_id, status, progress_pct,
235					  time_spent_mins, last_position, started_at, completed_at, synced_at
236		`, userID, req.ResourceID, req.PathID, req.Status, req.ProgressPct,
237			req.TimeSpentMin, req.LastPosition, startedAt, completedAt,
238		).Scan(
239			&p.ID, &p.UserID, &p.ResourceID, &p.PathID, &p.Status,
240			&p.ProgressPct, &p.TimeSpentMin, &p.LastPosition,
241			&p.StartedAt, &p.CompletedAt, &p.SyncedAt,
242		)
243		return p, err
244	}

418	func (r *LearningRepository) GetUserJobFamily(ctx context.Context, userID int) (string, error) {
419		var jf *string
420		err := r.db.QueryRow(ctx, "SELECT job_family FROM users WHERE id = $1", userID).Scan(&jf)
421		if err != nil || jf == nil {
422			return "", err
423		}
424		return *jf, nil
425	}
426	
427	// GetResourcesByJobFamily returns published resources targeting a specific job family.
428	func (r *LearningRepository) GetResourcesByJobFamily(ctx context.Context, jobFamily string) ([]models.Resource, error) {
429		rows, err := r.db.Query(ctx, `
430			SELECT DISTINCT r.id, r.title, r.category_id, r.popularity_score
431			FROM resources r
432			JOIN resource_tags rt ON rt.resource_id = r.id
433			JOIN taxonomy_tags t ON t.id = rt.tag_id
434			WHERE r.status = 'published'
435			AND (
436				t.name ILIKE '%' || $1 || '%'
437				OR EXISTS (
438					SELECT 1 FROM learning_paths lp
439					JOIN learning_path_items lpi ON lpi.path_id = lp.id
440					WHERE lpi.resource_id = r.id AND lp.target_job_family = $1
441				)
442			)
443			ORDER BY r.popularity_score DESC
444			LIMIT 50
445		`, jobFamily)
446		if err != nil {
447			return nil, err
448		}
449		defer rows.Close()
450	
451		resources := make([]models.Resource, 0)
452		for rows.Next() {
453			var r models.Resource
454			if err := rows.Scan(&r.ID, &r.Title, &r.CategoryID, &r.PopularityScore); err != nil {
455				return nil, err
456			}
457			resources = append(resources, r)
458		}
459		return resources, rows.Err()
460	}
461	
462	// GetAllPublishedResources returns all published resources with category info.
463	func (r *LearningRepository) GetAllPublishedResources(ctx context.Context) ([]models.Resource, error) {
464		rows, err := r.db.Query(ctx, `
465			SELECT id, title, category_id, popularity_score
466			FROM resources WHERE status = 'published'
467			ORDER BY popularity_score DESC
468		`)
469		if err != nil {
470			return nil, err
471		}
472		defer rows.Close()
473	
474		resources := make([]models.Resource, 0)
475		for rows.Next() {
476			var res models.Resource
477			if err := rows.Scan(&res.ID, &res.Title, &res.CategoryID, &res.PopularityScore); err != nil {
478				return nil, fmt.Errorf("scan resource: %w", err)
479			}
480			resources = append(resources, res)
481		}
482		return resources, rows.Err()
483	}

487	func scanPaths(rows pgx.Rows) ([]models.LearningPath, error) {
488		paths := make([]models.LearningPath, 0)
489		for rows.Next() {

493				&p.CategoryName, &p.TargetJobFamily, &p.RequiredCount, &p.ElectiveMin,

495				&p.CreatedAt, &p.UpdatedAt,
496			); err != nil {

498			}

1	package jwt
2	
3	import (
4		"errors"
5		"os"
6		"time"
7	
8		jwtlib "github.com/golang-jwt/jwt/v5"
9	)
10	
11	type Claims struct {
12		UserID      int      `json:"user_id"`
13		Username    string   `json:"username"`
14		Roles       []string `json:"roles"`
15		Permissions []string `json:"permissions"`
16		SessionID   string   `json:"session_id"`
17		jwtlib.RegisteredClaims
18	}
19	
20	var jwtSecret []byte
21	
22	func InitJWTSecret() error {
23		secret := os.Getenv("JWT_SECRET")
24		if secret == "" {
25			return errors.New("JWT_SECRET environment variable is required")
26		}
27		if len(secret) < 32 {
28			return errors.New("JWT_SECRET must be at least 32 characters")
29		}
30		jwtSecret = []byte(secret)
31		return nil
32	}
33	
34	// GenerateToken creates a signed JWT with user claims.
35	// The token itself is short-lived (30 min); session logic enforces idle/max lifetime separately.
36	func GenerateToken(userID int, username, sessionID string, roles, permissions []string) (string, error) {
37		now := time.Now()
38		claims := &Claims{
39			UserID:      userID,
40			Username:    username,
41			Roles:       roles,
42			Permissions: permissions,
43			SessionID:   sessionID,
44			RegisteredClaims: jwtlib.RegisteredClaims{
45				ExpiresAt: jwtlib.NewNumericDate(now.Add(30 * time.Minute)),
46				IssuedAt:  jwtlib.NewNumericDate(now),
47				NotBefore: jwtlib.NewNumericDate(now),
48				Issuer:    "wlpr-portal",
49			},
50		}
51	
52		token := jwtlib.NewWithClaims(jwtlib.SigningMethodHS256, claims)
53		return token.SignedString(jwtSecret)
54	}
55	
56	// ValidateToken parses and validates a JWT string, returning its claims.
57	func ValidateToken(tokenString string) (*Claims, error) {
58		token, err := jwtlib.ParseWithClaims(tokenString, &Claims{}, func(t *jwtlib.Token) (interface{}, error) {
59			if _, ok := t.Method.(*jwtlib.SigningMethodHMAC); !ok {
60				return nil, errors.New("unexpected signing method")
61			}
62			return jwtSecret, nil
63		})
64		if err != nil {
65			return nil, err
66		}
67	
68		claims, ok := token.Claims.(*Claims)
69		if !ok || !token.Valid {
70			return nil, errors.New("invalid token claims")
71		}
72	
73		return claims, nil
74	}
75	
76	// RefreshToken issues a new token with the same claims but a fresh expiry.
77	func RefreshToken(claims *Claims) (string, error) {
78		now := time.Now()
79		claims.ExpiresAt = jwtlib.NewNumericDate(now.Add(30 * time.Minute))
80		claims.IssuedAt = jwtlib.NewNumericDate(now)
81	
82		token := jwtlib.NewWithClaims(jwtlib.SigningMethodHS256, claims)
83		return token.SignedString(jwtSecret)
84	}
85

21	func (r *UserRepository) FindByUsername(ctx context.Context, username string) (*models.User, error) {

23		err := r.db.QueryRow(ctx, `
24			SELECT id, username, email, password_hash, display_name,
25				   mfa_enabled, mfa_secret_enc, mfa_recovery_enc,
26				   job_family, department, cost_center,
27				   is_active, failed_login_count, locked_until,
28				   last_login_at, created_at, updated_at
29			FROM users WHERE username = $1
30		`, username).Scan(
31			&user.ID, &user.Username, &user.Email, &user.PasswordHash, &user.DisplayName,
32			&user.MFAEnabled, &user.MFASecretEnc, &user.MFARecoveryEnc,
33			&user.JobFamily, &user.Department, &user.CostCenter,
34			&user.IsActive, &user.FailedLoginCount, &user.LockedUntil,
35			&user.LastLoginAt, &user.CreatedAt, &user.UpdatedAt,
36		)
37		if err == pgx.ErrNoRows {
38			return nil, nil
39		}
40		return user, err
41	}

50				   last_login_at, created_at, updated_at
51			FROM users WHERE id = $1
52		`, id).Scan(
53			&user.ID, &user.Username, &user.Email, &user.PasswordHash, &user.DisplayName,
54			&user.MFAEnabled, &user.MFASecretEnc, &user.MFARecoveryEnc,
55			&user.JobFamily, &user.Department, &user.CostCenter,
56			&user.IsActive, &user.FailedLoginCount, &user.LockedUntil,
57			&user.LastLoginAt, &user.CreatedAt, &user.UpdatedAt,

60			return nil, nil

332	// IsSessionValid checks idle timeout and max lifetime.
333	func IsSessionValid(session *models.Session) bool {
334		now := time.Now()
335		if session.Status != "active" {
336			return false
337		}
338		if now.After(session.ExpiresAt) {
339			return false
340		}
341		idleDeadline := session.LastActiveAt.Add(time.Duration(session.IdleTimeoutS) * time.Second)
342		if now.After(idleDeadline) {
343			return false
344		}
345		maxDeadline := session.CreatedAt.Add(time.Duration(session.MaxLifetimeS) * time.Second)
346		if now.After(maxDeadline) {
347			return false
348		}
349		return true
350	}

-- Auto-compute content_hash for near-duplicate detection
-- Uses SHA-256 of title + description + content_body
-- ============================================================

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION compute_content_hash()
RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
BEGIN
    NEW.content_hash := encode(
        digest(
            COALESCE(NEW.title, '') || '||' ||
            COALESCE(NEW.description, '') || '||' ||
            COALESCE(NEW.content_body, ''),
            'sha256'
        ),
        'hex'
    );
    RETURN NEW;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

CREATE TRIGGER trg_compute_content_hash
    BEFORE INSERT OR UPDATE OF title, description, content_body
    ON resources
    FOR EACH ROW
    EXECUTE FUNCTION compute_content_hash();

-- No seeded data: all content is created by users at runtime.


"}

			   r.duration_mins, r.difficulty, r.thumbnail_url, r.external_url,
			   r.view_count, r.completion_count, r.popularity_score,
			   r.published_at, r.created_at, r.updated_at
			   %s

		LEFT JOIN taxonomy_tags cat ON cat.id = r.category_id
		WHERE %s
		ORDER BY %s
		LIMIT %d OFFSET %d
	`, rankColumns, whereClause, orderClause, req.PageSize, offset)", "new_string": "	// Use a CTE to deduplicate near-duplicate resources by content_hash.
	// Within each content_hash group, keep the resource with the highest popularity_score.
	mainQuery := fmt.Sprintf(`
		WITH ranked AS (
			SELECT r.id, r.title, r.slug, r.description, r.resource_type, r.status,
				   r.category_id, COALESCE(cat.name, '') AS category_name,
				   r.duration_mins, r.difficulty, r.thumbnail_url, r.external_url,
				   r.view_count, r.completion_count, r.popularity_score,
				   r.published_at, r.created_at, r.updated_at,
				   r.content_hash
				   %s,
				   ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
					   PARTITION BY COALESCE(r.content_hash, r.id::text)
					   ORDER BY r.popularity_score DESC, r.id ASC
				   ) AS dedup_rank
			FROM resources r
			LEFT JOIN taxonomy_tags cat ON cat.id = r.category_id
			WHERE %s
		)
		SELECT id, title, slug, description, resource_type, status,
			   category_id, category_name,
			   duration_mins, difficulty, thumbnail_url, external_url,
			   view_count, completion_count, popularity_score,
			   published_at, created_at, updated_at,
			   search_rank, trgm_sim
		FROM ranked
		WHERE dedup_rank = 1
		ORDER BY %s
		LIMIT %d OFFSET %d
	`, rankColumns, whereClause, orderClause, req.PageSize, offset)"}

		WITH ranked AS (
			SELECT r.id, r.title, r.slug, r.description, r.resource_type, r.status,
				   r.category_id, COALESCE(cat.name, '') AS category_name,
				   r.duration_mins, r.difficulty, r.thumbnail_url, r.external_url,
				   r.view_count, r.completion_count, r.popularity_score,
				   r.published_at, r.created_at, r.updated_at,
				   r.content_hash
				   %s,
				   ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
					   PARTITION BY COALESCE(r.content_hash, r.id::text)
					   ORDER BY r.popularity_score DESC, r.id ASC
				   ) AS dedup_rank
			FROM resources r
			LEFT JOIN taxonomy_tags cat ON cat.id = r.category_id
			WHERE %s
		)
		SELECT id, title, slug, description, resource_type, status,
			   category_id, category_name,
			   duration_mins, difficulty, thumbnail_url, external_url,
			   view_count, completion_count, popularity_score,
			   published_at, created_at, updated_at,
			   search_rank, trgm_sim
		FROM ranked
		WHERE dedup_rank = 1
		ORDER BY %s
		LIMIT %d OFFSET %d
	`, rankColumns, whereClause, orderClause, req.PageSize, offset)"}

38	func jwtMiddleware(next echo.HandlerFunc) echo.HandlerFunc {
39		return func(c echo.Context) error {
40			authHeader := c.Request().Header.Get("Authorization")
41			if authHeader == "" {
42				return echo.NewHTTPError(http.StatusUnauthorized, "missing authorization header")
43			}
44			parts := strings.SplitN(authHeader, " ", 2)
45			if len(parts) != 2 || strings.ToLower(parts[0]) != "bearer" {
46				return echo.NewHTTPError(http.StatusUnauthorized, "invalid authorization format")
47			}
48			claims, err := jwt.ValidateToken(parts[1])
49			if err != nil {
50				return echo.NewHTTPError(http.StatusUnauthorized, "invalid token")
51			}
52			c.Set("claims", claims)
53			c.Set("user_id", claims.UserID)
54			c.Set("username", claims.Username)
55			c.Set("roles", claims.Roles)
56			c.Set("permissions", claims.Permissions)
57			c.Set("session_id", claims.SessionID)
58			return next(c)
59		}
60	}

76				}
77				return echo.NewHTTPError(http.StatusNotFound)
78			}
79		}

1	# WLPR Portal - Workforce Learning & Procurement Reconciliation
2	
3	A fully offline, on-premise enterprise portal for managing employee learning paths alongside vendor order governance and financial settlement. Built with Go (Echo), React (Vite), and PostgreSQL.
4	
5	## Architecture Overview
6	
7	```
8	┌─────────────┐     ┌──────────────────┐     ┌──────────────┐
9	│   React UI  │────▶│   Go/Echo API    │────▶│  PostgreSQL   │
10	│  (Nginx)    │     │   REST Backend   │     │   15+         │
11	│  Port 3000  │     │   Port 8080      │     │   Port 5432   │
12	└─────────────┘     └──────────────────┘     └──────────────┘
13	                           │
14	                    ┌──────┴──────┐
15	                    │  Background │
16	                    │   Workers   │
17	                    └─────────────┘
18	```
19	
20	### Key Subsystems
21	
22	- **Authentication & RBAC** - Local login with bcrypt hashing, optional TOTP MFA (AES-256 encrypted secrets), JWT tokens, session management (15m idle / 8h max), 6 roles with 18 granular permissions
23	- **Offline Full-Text Search** - PostgreSQL `tsvector` with weighted fields (A=title, B=description, C=body, D=pinyin), `pg_trgm` for typo tolerance, synonym expansion, pinyin matching for Chinese characters, multi-dimensional filtering
24	- **Recommendation Engine** - Background goroutine running on a 24h cycle computing user-item cosine similarity, job-family cold-start defaults, diversity control (max 40% from any single category)
25	- **Taxonomy** - Hierarchical job/skill tags with synonym conflict detection (DB trigger blocks two active synonyms pointing to different canonical tags), admin review queue with audit trails
26	- **Learning Paths** - Required/elective completion rules (e.g., 6 required + 2 electives), per-resource progress tracking with resume bookmarks, cross-device sync, CSV export
27	- **Reconciliation State Machine** - Invoice matching with auto-variance classification ($5.00 threshold), settlement transitions (open → matched → settled), AR/AP ledger with cost allocation by department/cost center, file-drop export to CSV
28	- **Dispute Workflow** - Created → Evidence Uploaded → Under Review → Arbitration → Resolved. Arbitration outcomes control review visibility: hidden, shown with disclaimer, or restored. DB trigger enforces valid state transitions
29	- **Configuration Center** - Feature flags with rollout strategies (all, role-based, percentage, disabled), in-memory cache with 30s background sync, app version compatibility checks
30	
31	## Access Points
32	
33	| Service  | URL                        | Description                    |
34	|----------|----------------------------|--------------------------------|
35	| Web UI   | http://localhost:3000       | React frontend served by Nginx |
36	| API      | http://localhost:8080       | Go/Echo REST API               |
37	| Database | localhost:5432              | PostgreSQL 15                  |
38	
39	## Getting Started: Registration
40	
41	The system ships with **no seeded users** and **no seeded content**. All data is created by users at runtime.
42	
43	1. **Register the first user** via the UI (`/register`) or API: `POST /api/auth/register`
44	   - The **first user** is automatically assigned the `system_admin` role with full access.
45	2. **Subsequent users** register normally and receive **no role** until an admin assigns one.
46	3. **Admin assigns roles** via the UI (Admin panel) or API: `POST /api/admin/users/assign-role`
47	
48	### Available Roles
49	
50	| Role                    | Access                                      |
[result-id: r38]

50	| Role                    | Access                                      |
51	|-------------------------|---------------------------------------------|
52	| `system_admin`          | Full access to all modules + admin panel     |
53	| `content_moderator`     | Learning library, taxonomy, content mgmt     |
54	| `learner`               | Learning paths, catalog, progress tracking   |
55	| `procurement_specialist`| Orders, reviews, disputes                    |
56	| `approver`              | Order/procurement approvals, reconciliation  |
57	| `finance_analyst`       | Reconciliation, settlements, cost allocation |
58	
59	### RBAC Invisibility
60	
61	Users without access to a feature **cannot discover it exists**:
62	- **Frontend**: Navigation menus and dashboard cards only show modules the user's role can access
63	- **Backend**: Unauthorized API requests return `404 Not Found` (not `403 Forbidden`)
64	- **Routes**: Frontend route guards render a generic 404 page for role-restricted pages
65	
66	## Quick Start
67

72	docker compose up --build -d
73	
74	# Watch logs
75	docker compose logs -f
76	
77	# Stop all services
78	docker compose down
79	
80	# Full reset (removes DB data)
81	docker compose down -v
82	docker volume prune -f
83	docker compose up --build -d
84	```
85	
86	### Local Development (without Docker)

100	go mod tidy

239	
240	## Configuration
241	
242	All configuration is managed through files in `config/` (no dot files):
243	
244	| File                  | Description                                      |
245	|-----------------------|--------------------------------------------------|
246	| `config/backend.env`  | Backend env vars (DB URL, JWT secret, AES key)   |
247	| `config/db.env`       | PostgreSQL credentials                           |
248	| `config/config.js`    | Frontend runtime config (injected via Docker vol) |
249	
250	Backend environment variables in `config/backend.env`:
251	
252	| Variable             | Default                                    | Description                          |
253	|----------------------|--------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------|
254	| `DATABASE_URL`       | `postgres://wlpr:wlpr_secret@...`          | PostgreSQL connection string         |
255	| `JWT_SECRET`         | *(required, min 32 chars)*                 | HMAC signing key for JWT tokens      |
256	| `AES_ENCRYPTION_KEY` | *(required, 64 hex chars = 32 bytes)*      | AES-256 key for TOTP secret encryption|
257	| `PORT`               | `8080`                                     | Backend listen port                  |
258	| `EXPORT_DIR`         | `/tmp/wlpr-exports`                        | Directory for file-drop CSV exports  |
259	
260	Frontend runtime config (`config/config.js`) is mounted into the Nginx container and loaded before the React app boots via `<script src="/config.js">`. Override at deploy time by replacing the file.
261	
262	## Running Tests
263	
264	```bash
265	# Full test suite (builds Docker, runs all tests, cleans up)
266	./run_test.sh          # macOS/Linux
267	./run_test.ps1         # Windows PowerShell
268	```
269	
270	The test runner executes:
271	1. Docker build (validates compilation)
272	2. Service health checks (backend API + frontend + config.js)
273	3. API smoke tests (login, search, learning, procurement, RBAC)
274	4. Integration tests against containerized PostgreSQL (dispute state machine, FTS triggers, synonym conflicts)
275	5. Unit tests (AES crypto, variance logic, taxonomy conflicts, API security)
276	6. Cleanup (docker compose down -v)
277	
278	## Security Notes
279	
280	- Passwords are hashed with bcrypt (cost 12). Accounts lock after 5 failed attempts for 15 minutes.
281	- TOTP MFA secrets are encrypted at rest using AES-256-GCM before storage in PostgreSQL.
282	- Sessions enforce both idle timeout (15 min default) and max lifetime (8 hours default), configurable via the admin panel.
283	- All API routes except login/MFA verify require a valid JWT with session validation.
284	- Role-based access control is enforced at the Echo middleware level — handlers never execute without passing RBAC checks.
285	- Dispute evidence metadata is encrypted at rest. Review content is masked in non-admin views based on arbitration outcomes.
286	- The `AES_ENCRYPTION_KEY` and `JWT_SECRET` values in `config/backend.env` are for development only. Replace them in production.
287

156	func TestIntegration_DisputeStateMachine_ArbitrationOutcomes(t *testing.T) {
157		db := getTestDB(t)
158		defer db.Close()
159		ctx := context.Background()
160	
161		outcomes := []string{"resolved_hidden", "resolved_disclaimer", "resolved_restored", "rejected"}
162	
163		for _, outcome := range outcomes {
164			t.Run("arbitration->"+outcome, func(t *testing.T) {
165				// Create fresh dispute for each outcome test
166				var vendorID, reviewID, disputeID int
167	
168				code := fmt.Sprintf("ARBO-%s-%d", outcome[:4], os.Getpid())
169				err := db.QueryRow(ctx, `
170					INSERT INTO vendors (name, code) VALUES ('ArbitTest', $1) RETURNING id
171				`, code).Scan(&vendorID)
172				if err != nil {
173					t.Fatalf("create vendor: %v", err)
174				}
175				defer db.Exec(ctx, "DELETE FROM vendors WHERE id = $1", vendorID)
176	
177				err = db.QueryRow(ctx, `
178					INSERT INTO vendor_reviews (vendor_id, reviewer_id, rating, body)
179					VALUES ($1, 1, 2, 'Test') RETURNING id
180				`, vendorID).Scan(&reviewID)
181				if err != nil {
182					t.Fatalf("create review: %v", err)
183				}
184				defer db.Exec(ctx, "DELETE FROM vendor_reviews WHERE id = $1", reviewID)
185	
186				err = db.QueryRow(ctx, `
187					INSERT INTO disputes (review_id, vendor_id, status, reason, created_by)
188					VALUES ($1, $2, 'created', 'Test', 1) RETURNING id
189				`, reviewID, vendorID).Scan(&disputeID)
190				if err != nil {
191					t.Fatalf("create dispute: %v", err)
192				}
193				defer db.Exec(ctx, "DELETE FROM disputes WHERE id = $1", disputeID)
194	
195				// Walk through valid path to arbitration
196				for _, transition := range []string{"evidence_uploaded", "under_review", "arbitration"} {
197					_, err = db.Exec(ctx, "UPDATE disputes SET status = $2 WHERE id = $1", disputeID, transition)
198					if err != nil {
199						t.Fatalf("transition to %s failed: %v", transition, err)
200					}
201				}
202	
203				// Apply the arbitration outcome
204				_, err = db.Exec(ctx, "UPDATE disputes SET status = $2 WHERE id = $1", disputeID, outcome)
205				if err != nil {
206					t.Fatalf("arbitration -> %s should be valid: %v", outcome, err)
207				}
208	
209				var finalStatus string
210				db.QueryRow(ctx, "SELECT status FROM disputes WHERE id = $1", disputeID).Scan(&finalStatus)
211				if finalStatus != outcome {
212					t.Errorf("expected %q, got %q", outcome, finalStatus)
213				}
214			})
215		}
216	}

**New files:**
1. `backend/internal/repository/scheduler_repo.go` - Scheduler repository

All 5 issues from the original audit are now fully addressed:

**Issue 1 (Near-duplicate dedup):** Content hash auto-computed via DB trigger. Search deduplicates via CTE with ROW_NUMBER partitioned by content_hash. Recommendations dedup before diversity control. Unit tests + integration tests.

**Issue 2 (Taxonomy review queue):** Tag/synonym creation routes through pending review queue with transactional audit logging. Moderator approve/reject endpoints activate/reject entities with audit trail. GetAllTags/hierarchy/children filter by active status. Full integration tests for the submit-review-approve/reject lifecycle.

**Issue 3 (Feature flag rollout):** IsFlagEnabled now requires userID + roleIDs. Percentage strategy uses deterministic FNV32a hash bucketing. Role-based strategy validates against actual role IDs from JWT. All call sites updated. 13 unit tests covering all strategies + distribution.

**Issue 4 (Scheduled jobs orchestrator):** SchedulerService reads from `scheduled_jobs` table, parses cron intervals, executes handlers with retry + exponential backoff, records all runs in `scheduled_job_runs`, runs compensation on exhausted retries. All 3 seeded jobs registered with handlers and compensators. Integration tests for run lifecycle.

**Issue 5 (Backend test path):** 42+ new unit test cases across 6 new test files. 13 new integration test cases across 3 new files. README updated with explicit non-Docker test commands. All tests designed to run without Docker.

content.name: Write
content.arguments:
{"file_path": "/Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t72/repo/docker-compose.yml", "content": "services:
  db:
    image: postgres:15-alpine
    container_name: wlpr-db
    environment:
      POSTGRES_DB: wlpr_portal
      POSTGRES_USER: wlpr
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: wlpr_secret
    ports:
      - "5432:5432"
    volumes:
      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data
      - ./backend/init.sql:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/01-init.sql
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U wlpr -d wlpr_portal"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 10
      start_period: 10s
    restart: unless-stopped

  backend:
    build: ./backend
    container_name: wlpr-backend
    ports:
      - "8080:8080"
    volumes:
      - ./config/config.js:/config/config.js:ro
    depends_on:
      db:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-f", "http://localhost:8080/api/health"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 15s
    restart: unless-stopped

  frontend:
    build: ./frontend
    container_name: wlpr-frontend
    ports:
      - "3000:80"
    volumes:
      - ./config/config.js:/usr/share/nginx/html/config.js:ro
    depends_on:
      backend:
        condition: service_healthy
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  pgdata:
"}

{"replace_all": false, "file_path": "/Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t72/repo/README.md", "old_string": "## Configuration

All configuration is managed through files in `config/` (no dot files):

| File                  | Description                                      |
|-----------------------|--------------------------------------------------|
| `config/backend.env`  | Backend env vars (DB URL, JWT secret, AES key)   |
| `config/db.env`       | PostgreSQL credentials                           |
| `config/config.js`    | Frontend runtime config (injected via Docker vol) |

Backend environment variables in `config/backend.env`:

| Variable             | Default                                    | Description                          |
|----------------------|--------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------|
| `DATABASE_URL`       | `postgres://wlpr:wlpr_secret@...`          | PostgreSQL connection string         |
| `JWT_SECRET`         | *(required, min 32 chars)*                 | HMAC signing key for JWT tokens      |
| `AES_ENCRYPTION_KEY` | *(required, 64 hex chars = 32 bytes)*      | AES-256 key for TOTP secret encryption|
| `PORT`               | `8080`                                     | Backend listen port                  |
| `EXPORT_DIR`         | `/tmp/wlpr-exports`                        | Directory for file-drop CSV exports  |

Frontend runtime config (`config/config.js`) is mounted into the Nginx container and loaded before the React app boots via `<script src="/config.js">`. Override at deploy time by replacing the file.", "new_string": "## Configuration

All configuration is managed through a single file: `config/config.js`. There are no `.env` files. Both the frontend and backend read from this one file.

| File                  | Description                                              |
|-----------------------|----------------------------------------------------------|
| `config/config.js`    | Single source of truth for all frontend + backend config |

### Configuration values in `config/config.js`:

| Key                  | Default                                    | Description                          |
|----------------------|--------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------|
| `DATABASE_URL`       | `postgres://wlpr:wlpr_secret@...`          | PostgreSQL connection string         |
| `JWT_SECRET`         | *(required, min 32 chars)*                 | HMAC signing key for JWT tokens      |
| `AES_ENCRYPTION_KEY` | *(required, 64 hex chars = 32 bytes)*      | AES-256 key for TOTP secret encryption|
| `PORT`               | `8080`                                     | Backend listen port                  |
| `POSTGRES_DB`        | `wlpr_portal`                              | PostgreSQL database name             |
| `POSTGRES_USER`      | `wlpr`                                     | PostgreSQL username                  |
| `POSTGRES_PASSWORD`  | `wlpr_secret`                              | PostgreSQL password                  |
| `API_BASE_URL`       | `/api`                                     | Frontend API base URL                |
| `APP_VERSION`        | `1.0.0`                                    | Client version for compatibility     |

The frontend loads `config.js` via `<script src="/config.js">` before the React app boots. The backend parses the same file at startup to extract database, JWT, and AES settings. Override at deploy time by mounting a replacement via Docker volume."}
